content.content:
1000	**Pagination `aria-current`:**
1001	
1002	- When Table includes pagination controls, the active page button MUST set `aria-current="page"` to indicate the current page to screen readers.
1003	- Other page buttons MUST NOT have `aria-current` set (omit the attribute entirely, do not set to `"false"`).
1004	
1005	### 3.2 Keyboard Interaction
1006	
1007	Grid keyboard navigation (when `interactive=true`):
1008	
1009	| Key          | Action                                                                                   |
1010	| ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1011	| `ArrowUp`    | Move focus to the cell above                                                             |
1012	| `ArrowDown`  | Move focus to the cell below                                                             |
1013	| `ArrowLeft`  | Move focus to the previous cell (wraps to previous row)                                  |
1014	| `ArrowRight` | Move focus to the next cell (wraps to next row)                                          |
1015	| `Home`       | Move focus to the first cell in the current row                                          |
1016	| `End`        | Move focus to the last cell in the current row                                           |
1017	| `Ctrl+Home`  | Move focus to the first cell in the first row                                            |
1018	| `Ctrl+End`   | Move focus to the last cell in the last row                                              |
1019	| `Enter`      | Activate the focused cell's primary interactive content                                  |
1020	| `F2`         | Enter edit mode for the focused cell (if editable)                                       |
1021	| `Escape`     | Exit edit mode, return focus to the cell                                                 |
1022	| `Space`      | Select/deselect the focused row (if selection enabled)                                   |
1023	| `Tab`        | Move focus to the next focusable element inside the cell; if none, move to the next cell |
1024	
1025	### 3.3 Focus Management
1026	
1027	- When `interactive=true`, the Table renders `role="grid"` and manages a roving `tabindex` across cells. The currently focused cell has `tabindex="0"`; all other cells have `tabindex="-1"`.
1028	- On initial focus (Tab into the grid), focus lands on the first cell of the first data row (or the last-focused cell if one was previously focused).
1029	- Arrow keys move focus between cells without wrapping out of the grid. `Home`/`End` wrap within the current row; `Ctrl+Home`/`Ctrl+End` move to the first/last cell of the first/last row.
1030	- When a cell contains interactive content (links, buttons, inputs), `Enter` activates it and `Escape` returns focus to the cell container.
1031	
1032	### 3.4 Screen Reader Announcements
1033	
1034	Sorting operations MUST be announced to assistive technology so screen reader users
1035	understand how the data is currently ordered.
1036	
1037	#### 3.4.1 `Messages` Sort Keys
1038	
1039	The `Messages` struct (passed via props or the i18n message bundle) includes the
1040	following sort-related keys:
1041	
1042	```rust
1043	/// Sort-related message keys within the Table's message bundle.
1044	/// All messages support ICU MessageFormat placeholders.
1045	pub struct SortMessages {
1046	    /// Announced when a column is sorted ascending.
1047	    /// Placeholder: `{column}` — the column's visible header text.
1048	    /// Default: "{column} sorted ascending"
1049	    pub sort_ascending: String,
1050	
1051	    /// Announced when a column is sorted descending.
1052	    /// Default: "{column} sorted descending"
1053	    pub sort_descending: String,
1054	
1055	    /// Announced when sort is removed from a column.
1056	    /// Default: "{column} sort removed"
1057	    pub sort_none: String,
1058	
1059	    /// Hint text for sortable column headers (used in `aria-roledescription`
1060	    /// or as a tooltip). Default: "activate to sort"
1061	    pub sortable_hint: String,
1062	
1063	    /// Announced when multi-column sort changes.
1064	    /// Placeholder: `{columns}` — comma-separated list of active sort columns
1065	    /// with their directions (e.g., "Name ascending, Date descending").
1066	    /// Default: "sorted by {columns}"
1067	    pub multi_sort_changed: String,
1068	}
1069	```
1070	
1071	#### 3.4.2 `aria-sort` Attribute
1072	
1073	Each sortable `<th>` element MUST carry the `aria-sort` attribute reflecting its current
1074	sort state:
1075	
1076	| Sort State | `aria-sort` Value |
1077	| ---------- | ----------------- |
1078	| Ascending  | `"ascending"`     |
1079	| Descending | `"descending"`    |
1080	| Unsorted   | `"none"`          |
1081	
1082	Non-sortable columns MUST NOT include `aria-sort`.
1083	
1084	#### 3.4.3 Live Region Announcement Timing
1085	
1086	When a sort operation completes (i.e., the data has been reordered and the DOM updated),
1087	the adapter MUST inject the appropriate message into an `aria-live="polite"` live region.
1088	The announcement fires immediately after the sort transition — no artificial delay.
1089	
1090	For single-column sort, the adapter uses `sort_ascending` or `sort_descending` with the
1091	column name substituted. When sort is removed, `sort_none` is used.
1092	
1093	For multi-column sort, the adapter uses `multi_sort_changed` with a formatted list of all
1094	active sort columns and their directions, ordered by sort priority (primary first).
1095	
1096	```rust
1097	/// Builds the live-region announcement string after a sort change.
1098	fn build_sort_announcement(
1099	    columns: &[ColumnDef],
1100	    sort_descriptors: &[SortDescriptor],
1101	    messages: &SortMessages,
1102	) -> String {
1103	    if sort_descriptors.is_empty() {
1104	        // All sorts removed — announce the last column that was unsorted.
1105	        return String::new();
1106	    }
1107	    if sort_descriptors.len() == 1 {
1108	        let desc = &sort_descriptors[0];
1109	        let col_name = columns.iter()
1110	            .find(|c| c.key == desc.column_key)
1111	            .map(|c| c.header_text.as_str())
1112	            .unwrap_or("");
1113	        return match desc.direction {
1114	            SortDirection::Ascending => (messages.sort_ascending)(&locale),
1115	            SortDirection::Descending => (messages.sort_descending)(&locale),
1116	        };
1117	    }
1118	    // Multi-column: build comma-separated list.
1119	    let parts = sort_descriptors.iter().map(|desc| {
1120	        let col_name = columns.iter()
1121	            .find(|c| c.key == desc.column_key)
1122	            .map(|c| c.header_text.as_str())
1123	            .unwrap_or("");
1124	        let dir = match desc.direction {
1125	            SortDirection::Ascending => "ascending",
1126	            SortDirection::Descending => "descending",
1127	        };
1128	        format!("{col_name} {dir}")
1129	    }).collect::<Vec<_>>();
1130	    messages.multi_sort_changed.replace("{columns}", &parts.join(", "))
1131	}
1132	```
1133	
1134	### 3.5 Virtual Scrolling
1135	
1136	When the Table uses virtual scrolling (only rendering visible rows), the `<table>` element must include `aria-colcount` and `aria-rowcount` attributes reflecting the total column and row counts (not just visible ones). Each visible `<tr>` must include `aria-rowindex` indicating its 1-based position in the full dataset.
1137	
1138	```rust,no_check
1139	// In table_attrs() when virtual scrolling is enabled:
1140	if self.ctx.virtual_scrolling {
1141	    attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::RowCount), self.ctx.total_rows.to_string());
1142	    attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::ColCount), self.ctx.total_cols.to_string());
1143	}
1144	
1145	// In row_attrs(index):
1146	if self.ctx.virtual_scrolling {
1147	    attrs.set(HtmlAttr::Aria(AriaAttr::RowIndex), (index + 1).to_string());
1148	}
1149	```
1150	
1151	> **Virtualizer Integration**: For large datasets, Table integrates with `Virtualizer` from `ars-collections` (§1.6 of `06-collections.md`). When `virtualizer: Option<Virtualizer>` is set in the Table context, only visible rows are rendered. The adapter wires scroll position updates to the Virtualizer, which computes the visible row range. A `ScrollToRow(Key)` event is available to programmatically scroll a specific row into view.
1152	
1153	#### 3.5.1 IntersectionObserver for Table Virtual Scroll
1154	
1155	When Table uses virtual scrolling for large datasets, the virtualizer MUST integrate with `IntersectionObserver` as specified in `06-collections.md` section "IntersectionObserver for Lazy Rendering & Virtual Scroll".
1156	
1157	**Sentinel Placement**: Within the Table's virtual scroll container, place sentinel elements at the top and bottom of the rendered row range. When a sentinel enters the viewport (with the configured `rootMargin` buffer), the virtualizer extends the rendered range in that direction.
1158	
1159	**Integration with AsyncCollection**: If Table's data source is an `AsyncCollection`, the bottom sentinel also triggers `load_more()` for infinite scroll / pagination. The sentinel must be placed AFTER the last rendered row but BEFORE the spacer element that maintains scroll height.
1160	
1161	**Cross-reference**: See `06-collections.md` for full `IntersectionObserver` configuration (root, rootMargin, threshold), sentinel element pattern, and cleanup requirements.
1162	
1163	## 4. Internationalization
1164	
1165	### 4.1 BiDi Isolation for Numeric and Currency Values
1166	
1167	Currency and numeric values in data display components must use BiDi isolation when rendered in mixed-direction contexts. Formatted values are wrapped in Unicode isolates (U+2066...U+2069) to prevent digit reordering in RTL layouts. Table cells containing numeric data should use `dir='ltr'` attribute to maintain correct digit order regardless of page direction.
1168	
1169	### 4.2 Ordinal Formatting
1170	
1171	Components displaying ordinal numbers (e.g., pagination "Page 1st", table ranking columns) must use ICU4X `PluralRules` with ordinal type for locale-correct formatting (English: 1st/2nd/3rd, French: 1er/2e, Japanese: 第1). An `format_ordinal(n: u64, locale: &Locale) -> String` utility is provided by `ars-i18n`. Components must not hardcode ordinal suffixes.
1172	
1173	- Cell values containing numbers use `number::Formatter` from `ars-i18n` for locale-aware
1174	  formatting. The Table machine does not format; adapters apply formatting at render time.
1175	- "Select all rows" and "Select row" labels are passed through `Messages`:
1176	
1177	```rust
1178	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
1179	pub struct Messages {
1180	    pub select_all: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1181	    pub select_row: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1182	    pub sort_ascending: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1183	    pub sort_descending: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1184	    pub sort_none: MessageFn<dyn Fn(&Locale) -> String + Send + Sync>,
1185	}
1186	impl Default for Messages {
1187	    fn default() -> Self {
1188	        Self {
1189	            select_all: MessageFn::static_str("Select all rows"),
1190	            select_row: MessageFn::static_str("Select row"),
1191	            sort_ascending: MessageFn::static_str("Sort ascending"),
1192	            sort_descending: MessageFn::static_str("Sort descending"),
1193	            sort_none: MessageFn::static_str("Remove sort"),
1194	        }
1195	    }
1196	}
1197	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
1198	```
1199	
1200	- **RTL layout**: In right-to-left locales the column order is visually reversed via CSS
1201	  `direction: rtl`. Sort direction indicators flip accordingly. The machine is RTL-agnostic;
1202	  the adapter applies `dir="rtl"` on the `Root` element based on the active locale.
1203	
1204	> **Drag and Drop Reorder**: When `dnd_enabled: true` is set, Table integrates with `DraggableCollection<T>` and `DroppableCollection<T>` from `06-collections.md` §7. Each row gains an optional `DragHandle` anatomy part, and `DropIndicator` parts appear between rows during drag. Fires `CollectionDndEvent::Reorder` on drop.
1205	
1206	### 4.3 Locale-Aware String Collation
1207	
1208	When Table sorts columns containing string data, sorting MUST use locale-aware collation rather than byte-order comparison. The `Collator` from `ars-i18n` (backed by ICU4X `Collator`) provides this capability.
1209	
1210	**Collation Levels:**
1211	
1212	| Level | Name               | Comparison Scope      | Example      |
1213	| ----- | ------------------ | --------------------- | ------------ |
1214	| 1     | Primary            | Base characters only  | a = á = A    |
1215	| 2     | Secondary          | Base + accents        | a = A, a ≠ á |
1216	| 3     | Tertiary (default) | Base + accents + case | a ≠ A, a ≠ á |
1217	
1218	**Props:**
1219	
1220	- `collation_level: CollationLevel` — optional prop on sortable columns. Defaults to `CollationLevel::Tertiary`.
1221	- The adapter passes the active locale to `Collator::new(locale, level)` when performing sort comparisons.
1222	
1223	**Cross-reference:** See `06-collections.md` for the `Collator` API and integration with `SortedCollection`.
1224	
1225	## 5. Variant: SelectAll
1226	
1227	When `selection_mode == Multiple`, the "Select All" checkbox in the header supports different
1228	strategies for datasets that may be paginated or virtualised.
1229	
1230	### 5.1 Additional Props
1231	
1232	```rust
1233	/// Controls what "Select All" means for the table.
1234	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
1235	pub enum SelectAllMode {
1236	    /// No select-all affordance is rendered.
1237	    None,
1238	    /// Select all currently visible (rendered) rows. This is the default.
1239	    AllVisible,
1240	    /// Select all rows in the dataset, including those not yet loaded.
1241	    /// `total_count` is the known cardinality — used for the checkbox label
1242	    /// ("Select all 1,204 items") and for the `aria-checked` derivation.
1243	    AllData { total_count: usize },
1244	}
1245	```
1246	
1247	### 5.2 Behavior
1248	
1249	**Exclusion-set pattern** — The `selection::Set::All` variant from `ars_collections` natively
1250	supports the "select all with exclusions" pattern. When the user selects all via `AllData`
1251	and then deselects individual rows, `selection::State::deselect()` transitions from `All` to
1252	`Keys(complement)` using the full known key set. For async/paginated collections where the
1253	full key set is unknown, the adapter should track an exclusion set alongside `selection::Set::All`:
1254	
1255	```rust
1256	/// Extended selection tracking for server-paginated AllData tables.
1257	/// Wraps the canonical `selection::Set` with an exclusion list for rows
1258	/// deselected after a bulk select-all on an incompletely-loaded collection.
1259	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
1260	pub struct BulkSelection {
1261	    /// Row IDs explicitly deselected after a `selection::Set::All` selection.
1262	    /// Only meaningful when `selected_rows` is `selection::Set::All`.
1263	    pub excluded: BTreeSet<Key>,
1264	}
1265	
1266	impl BulkSelection {
1267	    /// Checks if a row is selected.
1268	    pub fn is_selected(&self, row_id: &Key, selection: &selection::Set) -> bool {
1269	        match selection {
1270	            selection::Set::All => !self.excluded.contains(row_id),
1271	            other => other.contains(row_id),
1272	        }
1273	    }
1274	
1275	    /// Returns the effective count of selected rows.
1276	    pub fn effective_count(&self, total: usize, selection: &selection::Set) -> usize {
1277	        match selection {
1278	            selection::Set::All => total.saturating_sub(self.excluded.len()),
1279	            selection::Set::Multiple(s) => s.len(),
1280	            selection::Set::Empty => 0,
1281	        }
1282	    }
1283	}
1284	```
1285	
1286	When `selection::Set::All` is active and the user deselects a row via `DeselectRow`, the row ID is
1287	added to `excluded`. The `SelectAllCheckbox` shows `aria-checked="mixed"` when `excluded` is
1288	non-empty and selection is `All`.
1289	
1290	### 5.3 Accessibility
1291	
1292	The adapter communicates the bulk selection state to the server for operations like "delete
1293	selected" — sending `{ select_all: true, excluded: [...] }` is far more efficient than
1294	enumerating potentially millions of selected row IDs.
1295	
1296	## 6. Variant: Column Resizing
1297	
1298	Table supports user-driven column resizing via drag handles between column headers.
1299	
1300	### 6.1 Additional Events
1301	
1302	```rust,no_check
1303	/// Column resize event — added to the Table Event enum.
1304	ColumnResize { column: String, width: f64 },
1305	```
1306	
1307	### 6.2 Additional Context
1308	
1309	```rust,no_check
1310	/// Added to the Table Context struct.
1311	/// Map from column ID to current width in pixels. `None` means auto-width.
1312	pub column_widths: BTreeMap<String, f64>,
1313	/// Column currently being resized (for drag state).
1314	pub resizing_column: Option<String>,
1315	```
1316	
1317	### 6.3 Anatomy Additions
1318	
1319	The `ColumnResizeHandle` anatomy part is a child of each `ColumnHeader`:
1320	
1321	```text
1322	ColumnHeader  (<th scope="col">)
1323	├── ... (existing children)
1324	└── ColumnResizeHandle  (<div>; data-ars-part="column-resize-handle")
1325	```
1326	
1327	| Part                 | Element | Key Attributes                                                                                                                                                |
1328	| -------------------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
1329	| `ColumnResizeHandle` | `<div>` | `data-ars-part="column-resize-handle"`, `data-ars-resizing` (present during drag), `role="separator"`, `aria-orientation="vertical"`, `aria-valuenow={width}` |
1330	
1331	### 6.4 Behavior
1332	
1333	- **Pointer drag**: `pointerdown` on the handle starts resize. `pointermove` updates
1334	  `column_widths[column]`. `pointerup` commits.
1335	- **Keyboard**: When focused, `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight` adjust width by `step` (default 10px).
1336	  `aria-valuenow` reflects the current pixel width.
1337	- **Constraint**: `min_column_width` prop (default 50px) prevents columns from collapsing to zero.
1338	- The adapter applies `style="width: {w}px"` to each `<col>` in a `<colgroup>` element.
1339	
1340	## 7. Library Parity
1341	
1342	> Compared against: React Aria (`Table`).
1343	
1344	### 7.1 Props
1345	
1346	| Feature                                   | ars-ui                             | React Aria                   | Notes                                                      |
1347	| ----------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
1348	| `selection_mode`                          | `selection::Mode`                  | `SelectionMode`              | Equivalent                                                 |
1349	| `selected_rows` / `default_selected_rows` | `selection::Set`                   | `Iterable<Key> \| 'all'`     | ars-ui uses canonical `selection::Set` with `All` variant  |
1350	| `sort_descriptor`                         | `Bindable<Option<SortDescriptor>>` | `SortDescriptor`             | ars-ui adds controlled/uncontrolled via Bindable           |
1351	| `disabled_keys`                           | `BTreeSet<Key>`                    | `Iterable<Key>`              | Added from React Aria                                      |
1352	| `disallow_empty_selection`                | `bool`                             | `bool`                       | Equivalent                                                 |
1353	| `escape_key_behavior`                     | `EscapeKeyBehavior`                | `'none' \| 'clearSelection'` | Added from React Aria                                      |
1354	| `selection_behavior`                      | `selection::Behavior`              | `SelectionBehavior`          | Equivalent                                                 |
1355	| `interactive`                             | `bool`                             | --                           | ars-ui original (controls `role="grid"` vs `role="table"`) |
1356	| `sticky_header`                           | `bool`                             | --                           | ars-ui original                                            |
1357	| `caption`                                 | `Option<String>`                   | --                           | ars-ui original                                            |
1358	| `expanded_rows`                           | `Bindable<BTreeSet<Key>>`          | --                           | ars-ui original                                            |
1359	| `drag_and_drop_hooks`                     | Via `06-collections.md`            | `DragAndDropHooks`           | Both support DnD                                           |
1360	| `disabledBehavior`                        | --                                 | `DisabledBehavior`           | Omitted; ars-ui disabled rows are fully non-interactive    |
1361	
1362	**Gaps:** None.
1363	
1364	### 7.2 Anatomy
1365	
1366	| Part               | ars-ui                             | React Aria                | Notes                                                      |
1367	| ------------------ | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
1368	| Root wrapper       | `Root`                             | --                        | ars-ui adds a wrapper div                                  |
1369	| Table              | `Table`                            | `Table`                   | --                                                         |
1370	| Header             | `Head`                             | `TableHeader`             | --                                                         |
1371	| Column             | `ColumnHeader`                     | `Column`                  | --                                                         |
1372	| ColumnResizer      | `ColumnResizeHandle` (§6)          | `ColumnResizer`           | --                                                         |
1373	| Body               | `Body`                             | `TableBody`               | --                                                         |
1374	| Row                | `Row`                              | `Row`                     | --                                                         |
1375	| Cell               | `Cell`                             | `Cell`                    | --                                                         |
1376	| Footer             | `Foot`                             | --                        | ars-ui original                                            |
1377	| Caption            | `Caption`                          | --                        | ars-ui original                                            |
1378	| RowHeader          | `RowHeader`                        | --                        | ars-ui original                                            |
1379	| Selection controls | `SelectAllCheckbox`, `RowCheckbox` | `Checkbox`                | ars-ui splits into explicit parts                          |
1380	| Expand             | `ExpandTrigger`, `ExpandedContent` | --                        | ars-ui original                                            |
1381	| Load more          | via `on_load_more` + §1.6          | `TableLoadMoreItem`       | Both support infinite scroll                               |
1382	| ResizableContainer | --                                 | `ResizableTableContainer` | ars-ui handles resizing via §6 without a wrapper component |
1383	
1384	**Gaps:** None.
1385	
1386	### 7.3 Events
1387	
1388	| Callback                                          | ars-ui                               | React Aria                                   | Notes                 |
1389	| ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------- | --------------------- |
1390	| `on_selection_change`                             | Adapter layer (Bindable observation) | `onSelectionChange`                          | Equivalent            |
1391	| `on_sort_change`                                  | Adapter layer                        | `onSortChange`                               | Equivalent            |
1392	| `on_row_action`                                   | `RowAction` event                    | `onRowAction`                                | Added from React Aria |
1393	| `on_resize` / `on_resize_start` / `on_resize_end` | Via §6 ColumnResize event            | `onResize` / `onResizeStart` / `onResizeEnd` | Equivalent            |
1394	
1395	**Gaps:** None.
1396	
1397	### 7.4 Features
1398	
1399	| Feature                   | ars-ui                | React Aria                |
1400	| ------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------- |
1401	| Single/multiple selection | Yes                   | Yes                       |
1402	| Sorting                   | Yes                   | Yes                       |
1403	| Column resizing           | Yes (§6)              | Yes                       |
1404	| Disabled rows             | Yes                   | Yes                       |
1405	| Row expansion             | Yes                   | No                        |
1406	| Sticky header             | Yes                   | No                        |
1407	| Virtual scrolling         | Yes (§3.5)            | Yes                       |
1408	| Infinite scroll           | Yes (`on_load_more`)  | Yes (`TableLoadMoreItem`) |
1409	| Drag and drop             | Yes (via collections) | Yes (`dragAndDropHooks`)  |
1410	| Grid keyboard nav         | Yes (`interactive`)   | Yes                       |
1411	| Row links (`href`)        | Yes                   | Yes                       |
1412	
1413	**Gaps:** None.
1414	
1415	### 7.5 Summary
1416	
1417	- **Overall:** Full parity.
1418	- **Divergences:** React Aria's `disabledBehavior` (controlling whether disabled items remain focusable) is omitted; ars-ui treats disabled rows as fully non-interactive. React Aria uses a wrapper `ResizableTableContainer` component for column resizing; ars-ui integrates resizing directly via the §6 variant.
1419	- **Recommended additions:** None.

100	
101	    /// Move focus to the next non-disabled tab in DOM order.
102	    FocusNext,
103	
104	    /// Move focus to the previous non-disabled tab in DOM order.
105	    FocusPrev,
106	
107	    /// Move focus to the first non-disabled tab.
108	    FocusFirst,
109	
110	    /// Move focus to the last non-disabled tab.
111	    FocusLast,
112	
113	    /// Replace [`Context::dir`] with the supplied [`Direction`]. Sources:
114	    ///
115	    /// - The adapter dispatches this once on mount with the resolved
116	    ///   concrete value when [`Props::dir`] was [`Direction::Auto`].
117	    /// - [`Machine::on_props_changed`] dispatches this when [`Props::dir`]
118	    ///   changes between renders (including `Concrete → Auto`, which the
119	    ///   consumer uses to ask the adapter to re-resolve from the platform).
120	    ///
121	    /// Idempotent — sending the same direction twice produces no
122	    /// transition.
123	    SetDirection(Direction),
124	
125	    /// Replace the registered tab list. Adapters call this whenever their
126	    /// rendered tab triggers change (mount, unmount, reorder, close).
127	    /// The transition replaces [`Context::tabs`] and [`Context::closable_tabs`]
128	    /// in one shot — duplicate keys are deduplicated (the first occurrence
129	    /// wins) — then snaps [`Context::value`] / [`Context::focused_tab`]
130	    /// back into the new list (see §1.5 selection invariant).
131	    SetTabs(Vec<TabRegistration>),
132	
133	    /// Re-apply context-backed [`Props`] fields after a prop change.
134	    /// Adapters dispatch this from [`Machine::on_props_changed`] when
135	    /// any of `orientation`, `activation_mode`, `loop_focus`, or
136	    /// `disabled_keys` differs between old and new props. After a
137	    /// `disabled_keys` change the transition also re-runs the selection
138	    /// invariant — `value` / `focused_tab` snap to the first non-disabled
139	    /// key when they now point at a disabled tab. State downgrades from
140	    /// [`State::Focused`] to [`State::Idle`] when the focused tab is no
141	    /// longer registered or has just been disabled.
142	    ///
143	    /// `dir` is intentionally excluded — direction changes are dispatched
144	    /// as [`Event::SetDirection`] by [`Machine::on_props_changed`] so
145	    /// that an unrelated prop delta cannot clobber a runtime-resolved
146	    /// direction installed via [`Event::SetDirection`].
147	    SyncProps,
148	
149	    /// Re-resolve provider-backed locale and message bundle data after the
150	    /// surrounding adapter provider changes.
151	    ///
152	    /// The machine is created once, but adapters may render inside an
153	    /// `ArsProvider` whose locale signal changes at runtime. Close-button
154	    /// labels and reorder announcements read from [`Context::messages`] /
155	    /// [`Context::locale`], so adapters dispatch this event whenever the live
156	    /// provider messages differ from the machine's current snapshot.
157	    SyncMessages {
158	        /// Active provider locale.
159	        locale: Locale,
160	
161	        /// Localized tab messages for `locale`.
162	        messages: Messages,
163	    },
164	
165	    /// User asked to close the given tab. Closable variant (spec §5).
166	    ///
167	    /// **Pure notification** — the machine does NOT mutate
168	    /// [`Context::tabs`] or [`Context::value`]. Consumers call
169	    /// [`Api::successor_for_close`] / [`Api::can_close_tab`] from their
170	    /// `CloseTab` handler to apply the deterministic successor algorithm
171	    /// to their own tab-list source, then dispatch
172	    /// [`Event::SetTabs`] / [`Event::SelectTab`] to commit the change.
173	    CloseTab(Key),
174	
175	    /// User asked to move the given tab to a new index. Reorderable variant
176	    /// (spec §6).
177	    ///
178	    /// **Pure notification** — per spec §6.3 the machine does NOT mutate
179	    /// [`Context::tabs`]. Consumers reorder their tab-list source and
180	    /// re-register via [`Event::SetTabs`].
181	    ReorderTab {
182	        /// The key of the tab being moved.
183	        tab: Key,
184	
185	        /// The target zero-based index in the tab list.
186	        new_index: usize,
187	    },
188	
189	    /// Push a new controlled value into [`Context::value`] via
190	    /// [`Bindable::sync_controlled`].
191	    ///
192	    /// Adapters dispatch this from [`Machine::on_props_changed`] when
193	    /// [`Props::value`] changes between renders. The transition reapplies
194	    /// the selection invariant (snapping the focused tab if the new
195	    /// controlled key is disabled or unregistered) and downgrades
196	    /// `Focused → Idle` when the snap clears the focused tab.
197	    ///
198	    /// `None` is the controlled "no tab selected" value. It does not switch
199	    /// the instance into uncontrolled mode; controlled/uncontrolled mode is
200	    /// fixed by [`Props::value`] at mount.
201	    SyncControlledValue(Option<Key>),
202	}
203	
204	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
205	// ActivationMode
206	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
207	
208	/// How keyboard focus interacts with selection.
209	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
210	pub enum ActivationMode {
211	    /// Focusing a tab via keyboard immediately selects it.
212	    #[default]
213	    Automatic,
214	
215	    /// Focusing a tab via keyboard only moves the focus ring; the user
216	    /// must press `Enter` or `Space` to confirm selection.
217	    Manual,
218	}
219	
220	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
221	// TabRegistration
222	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
223	
224	/// Adapter-supplied registration entry for a single tab.
225	///
226	/// Used as the payload of [`Event::SetTabs`] so a single bulk-replace
227	/// dispatch atomically updates [`Context::tabs`] and
228	/// [`Context::closable_tabs`]. Tab labels are intentionally NOT included —
229	/// labels are a render concern owned by the adapter / consumer.
230	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
231	pub struct TabRegistration {
232	    /// Stable identifier for the tab. Must be unique within the list.
233	    pub key: Key,
234	
235	    /// When `true`, adapters render a close button inside this tab and
236	    /// the agnostic core forwards `Delete` / `Backspace` keystrokes as
237	    /// [`Event::CloseTab`]. Non-closable tabs ignore the keystrokes.
238	    pub closable: bool,
239	}
240	
241	impl TabRegistration {
242	    /// Builds a registration for a non-closable tab.
243	    #[must_use]
244	    pub const fn new(key: Key) -> Self {
245	        Self {
246	            key,
247	            closable: false,
248	        }
249	    }
250	
251	    /// Builds a registration for a closable tab.
252	    #[must_use]
253	    pub const fn closable(key: Key) -> Self {
254	        Self {
255	            key,
256	            closable: true,
257	        }
258	    }
259	}
260	
261	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
262	// Adapter metadata helpers
263	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
264	
265	/// Framework-agnostic metadata for one rendered adapter tab row.
266	///
267	/// Adapters keep the actual trigger and panel render values in their own
268	/// `Tab` structs (`ViewFn` for Leptos, `Element` for Dioxus), but both
269	/// adapters need the same semantic row data to register tabs, aggregate
270	/// disabled keys, look typed keys back up from internal [`Key`] values, and
271	/// plan drag reorders. This type centralizes that shared, DOM-free slice.
272	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
273	pub struct TabMeta<K: TabKey> {
274	    /// Typed tab identifier supplied by the adapter user.
275	    pub typed_key: K,
276	
277	    /// Internal machine key derived from [`typed_key`](Self::typed_key).
278	    pub key: Key,
279	
280	    /// Resolved semantic label used for close-button names and reorder
281	    /// announcements.
282	    pub label_text: String,
283	
284	    /// Whether the tab renders a close affordance and accepts close
285	    /// keyboard shortcuts.
286	    pub closable: bool,
287	
288	    /// Whether the tab is disabled after merging per-row and prop-level
289	    /// disabled state.
290	    pub disabled: bool,
291	}
292	
293	impl<K: TabKey> TabMeta<K> {
294	    /// Builds metadata for one adapter-rendered tab row.
295	    #[must_use]
296	    pub fn new(
297	        typed_key: K,
298	        label_text: impl Into<String>,
299	        closable: bool,
300	        disabled: bool,
301	    ) -> Self {
302	        Self {
303	            typed_key,
304	            key: typed_key.into_key(),
305	            label_text: label_text.into(),
306	            closable,
307	            disabled,
308	        }
309	    }
310	}
311	
312	/// Adapter-level reorder callback payload.
313	///
314	/// The payload is typed with the public adapter key rather than the
315	/// internal [`Key`] so consumers using enum-backed tabs cannot accidentally
316	/// mix key domains in their callback handling.
317	#[derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
318	pub struct ReorderEvent<K: TabKey> {
319	    /// Tab being moved.
320	    pub key: K,
321	
322	    /// Current zero-based index before the consumer applies the reorder.
323	    pub old_index: usize,
324	
325	    /// Requested zero-based index after the reorder.
326	    pub new_index: usize,
327	}
328	
329	impl<K: TabKey> Debug for ReorderEvent<K> {
330	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
331	        f.debug_struct("ReorderEvent")
332	            .field("key", &self.key.into_key())
333	            .field("old_index", &self.old_index)
334	            .field("new_index", &self.new_index)
335	            .finish()
336	    }
337	}
338	
339	/// Fully-resolved plan for a drag reorder between two rendered tab rows.
340	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
341	pub struct ReorderPlan<K: TabKey> {
342	    /// Typed callback payload to send before committing the reorder.
343	    pub event: ReorderEvent<K>,
344	
345	    /// Semantic label of the moved tab, used for the live announcement.
346	    pub label_text: String,
347	
348	    /// Total number of registered tabs at the time of the reorder.
349	    pub total: usize,
350	}
351	
352	/// Builds the registration list dispatched to the agnostic core.
353	#[must_use]
354	pub fn registrations_from_meta<K: TabKey>(meta: &[TabMeta<K>]) -> Vec<TabRegistration> {
355	    meta.iter()
356	        .map(|tab| TabRegistration {
357	            key: tab.key.clone(),
358	            closable: tab.closable,
359	        })
360	        .collect()
361	}
362	
363	/// Builds the disabled-key set from a metadata snapshot.
364	#[must_use]
365	pub fn disabled_keys_from_meta<K: TabKey>(meta: &[TabMeta<K>]) -> BTreeSet<Key> {
366	    meta.iter()
367	        .filter(|tab| tab.disabled)
368	        .map(|tab| tab.key.clone())
369	        .collect()
370	}
371	
372	/// Finds the typed adapter key for an internal machine key.
373	#[must_use]
374	pub fn typed_key_for_key<K: TabKey>(meta: &[TabMeta<K>], key: &Key) -> Option<K> {
375	    meta.iter()
376	        .find(|tab| tab.key == *key)
377	        .map(|tab| tab.typed_key)
378	}
379	
380	/// Builds a drag reorder plan from rendered source and target keys.
381	///
382	/// Disabled rows cannot be dragged and cannot receive drops, so this
383	/// returns `None` when either endpoint is disabled or missing.
384	#[must_use]
385	pub fn drag_reorder_plan<K: TabKey>(
386	    meta: &[TabMeta<K>],
387	    source_key: &Key,
388	    target_key: &Key,
389	) -> Option<ReorderPlan<K>> {
390	    let old_index = meta
391	        .iter()
392	        .position(|tab| tab.key == *source_key && !tab.disabled)?;
393	
394	    let new_index = meta
395	        .iter()
396	        .position(|tab| tab.key == *target_key && !tab.disabled)?;
397	
398	    let moved = meta.get(old_index)?;
399	
400	    Some(ReorderPlan {
401	        event: ReorderEvent {
402	            key: moved.typed_key,
403	            old_index,
404	            new_index,
405	        },
406	        label_text: moved.label_text.clone(),
407	        total: meta.len(),
408	    })
409	}
410	
411	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
412	// Messages
413	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
414	
415	/// Closure signature backing [`Messages::close_tab_label`].
416	///
417	/// Receives the parent tab's visible label and the active locale, and
418	/// returns the accessible name rendered on the close button
419	/// (e.g. `"Close Inbox"`).
420	pub type CloseTabLabelFn = dyn Fn(&str, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
421	
422	/// Closure signature backing [`Messages::reorder_announce_label`].
423	///
424	/// Receives the moved tab's visible label, the new 1-based position,
425	/// the total tab count, and the active locale, and returns the
426	/// `LiveAnnouncer` announcement text
427	/// (e.g. `"Inbox moved to position 2 of 5"`).
428	pub type ReorderAnnounceLabelFn = dyn Fn(&str, usize, usize, &Locale) -> String + Send + Sync;
429	
430	/// Localizable strings for [`Tabs`](self).
431	///
432	/// `close_tab_label` is the accessible name for the close button rendered
433	/// inside a closable tab (default English template: `"Close {label}"`).
434	/// `reorder_announce_label` is the `LiveAnnouncer` text emitted by adapters
435	/// after a keyboard reorder (default: `"{label} moved to position {n} of {total}"`).
436	/// The agnostic core never invokes `reorder_announce_label` itself —
437	/// announcing is an adapter concern — but exposing it on [`Messages`]
438	/// keeps i18n centralized.
439	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
440	pub struct Messages {
441	    /// Builds the accessible name for a tab's close button. See
442	    /// [`CloseTabLabelFn`] for the closure signature.
443	    pub close_tab_label: MessageFn<CloseTabLabelFn>,
444	
445	    /// Builds the `LiveAnnouncer` text emitted after a keyboard-driven
446	    /// reorder. See [`ReorderAnnounceLabelFn`] for the closure
447	    /// signature.
448	    pub reorder_announce_label: MessageFn<ReorderAnnounceLabelFn>,
449	}
450	
451	impl Default for Messages {
452	    fn default() -> Self {
453	        Self {
454	            close_tab_label: MessageFn::new(|label: &str, _locale: &Locale| {
455	                format!("Close {label}")
456	            }),
457	            reorder_announce_label: MessageFn::new(
458	                |label: &str, position: usize, total: usize, _locale: &Locale| {
459	                    format!("{label} moved to position {position} of {total}")
460	                },
461	            ),
462	        }
463	    }
464	}
465	
466	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
467	
468	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
469	// Props
470	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
471	
472	/// Immutable configuration for a [`Tabs`](self) instance.
473	///
474	/// `Props` is constructed once by the consumer; runtime updates that drive
475	/// [`Context`] should be replicated by sending the matching [`Event`]
476	/// (e.g. [`Event::SetDirection`] for [`Direction`] resolution,
477	/// [`Event::SetTabs`] for tab-list changes).
478	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, ars_core::HasId)]
479	pub struct Props {
480	    /// Component instance id. Required (used as the prefix for the
481	    /// generated `tablist` DOM id).
482	    pub id: String,
483	
484	    /// Controlled selected-tab key. When `Some`, overrides
485	    /// [`default_value`](Self::default_value); the consumer is responsible
486	    /// for syncing changes via [`Bindable::sync_controlled`]. The outer
487	    /// `Option` represents "no tab selected" (e.g. an empty tab list).
488	    pub value: Option<Option<Key>>,
489	
490	    /// Initial selected-tab key in uncontrolled mode. `None` means the
491	    /// component boots with no selection (typical for empty / lazy tab
492	    /// lists).
493	    pub default_value: Option<Key>,
494	
495	    /// Tab list orientation. Default [`Orientation::Horizontal`].
496	    pub orientation: Orientation,
497	
498	    /// How keyboard focus interacts with selection. Default
499	    /// [`ActivationMode::Automatic`].

=== RUST SKILLS DISPLAY FORMAT ===
When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:
1. FIRST: "🦀 Rust Skills Loaded" text
2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

=== MANDATORY: META-COGNITION ROUTING ===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

| Keywords in Question | Domain Skill to Load |
|---------------------|---------------------|
| Web API, HTTP, REST, axum, actix, handler, router | domain-web |
| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

input.content:
# Task #286 — Implement Table agnostic core

## Context

Task **#286** ("Implement Table agnostic core", 8 pts, complex tier) is the
framework-agnostic state machine for the `Table` data-display component. The
Collections Epic (#53) it depends on closed in 2026-04-08, so it is unblocked.

Spec: `spec/components/data-display/table.md` (1419 lines).

The Table is a stateful machine that owns row/column selection, sort
descriptors, expansion, grid focus, optional column-resize state, and
optional virtual-scrolling metadata. The agnostic core MUST NOT touch the
DOM — adapters apply focus, measure layout, and observe scroll. The core
produces ARIA + `data-ars-*` attributes via `ConnectApi` and the typed
`Api<'_>` so both Leptos and Dioxus adapters can render identical markup.

### Spec drift to fix in this task

The spec is internally inconsistent in three places that the implementation
must reconcile (per the CLAUDE.md rule "Resolve the spec/code mismatch in
the same task"):

1. **`Context::dir: Direction`** is missing from §1.3 but referenced by
   `Api::on_cell_keydown` (line 770) for RTL arrow-key resolution. Add the
   field and a `Direction::Auto → concrete` resolution in `init` via
   `env.locale`, plus a `SetDirection(Direction)` event so runtime
   direction changes are wired through.
2. **`Context::is_sorting: bool`** is described in §1.1.1 ("Visual
   Feedback") but absent from the §1.3 Context struct. Add it. Toggle it
   inside the `SortColumn` arm.
3. **`build_sort_announcement` (§3.4.3)** references undeclared `locale`
   and uses `SortDescriptor::column_key` instead of `column`. This is
   illustrative adapter code, not part of the agnostic core — leave the
   spec drift note for a follow-up spec-only PR (no impl impact here).

Items (1) and (2) are spec edits in the same PR; (3) is documented in the
PR body but not fixed.

## Approach

Implement the Table machine + connect API as a single Rust module living
in a `table/` directory next to existing data-display components, with
unit + snapshot tests inline via `mod tests;`. Use **Tabs** as the
structural template (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/`) — it is
the closest existing complex component (registered keys + selection +
keyboard nav + Bindable controlled/uncontrolled + Direction).

### Variants covered in this task

The issue's "Tests to add first" list names every variant explicitly:

- §5 SelectAll (mixed checkbox, `selection::Set::All`) → included
- §6 Column Resizing (`ColumnResizeHandle` part, `ColumnResize` event,
  `column_widths` + `resizing_column` in Context) → included
- §3.5 Virtual Scrolling (`virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
  `aria-rowcount`/`aria-colcount` + `aria-rowindex`) → included
- §1.6 Async data loading (`loading: Bindable<bool>`, minimal
  `SetLoading(bool)` event) → included (Bindable field + event; the
  `on_load_more` callback is adapter-only and is not stored in the core)
- §5 `BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper → **excluded**. The spec
  describes it as adapter/application code that wraps the canonical
  `selection::Set::All`; it is not part of the agnostic core.

### Files to create

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/mod.rs` | State, Event, Context, Props, Messages, Machine impl, Part enum, Api with all `*_attrs` and keyboard helpers |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/tests.rs` | Inline unit + snapshot tests for every named issue test |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/snapshots/` | insta `.snap` fixtures (auto-created) |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/data_display.rs` | Add `assert_anatomy` entry for Table (file may not yet exist — create) |

### Files to modify

| Path | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/mod.rs` | Add `pub mod table;` |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance.rs` | Register new `data_display` module if not present |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs` | Add `proptest_table_event_sequences_preserve_invariants` (selection ⊆ rows, expansion ⊆ rows, focus in bounds) |
| `spec/components/data-display/table.md` | §1.3 Context: add `dir: Direction`, `is_sorting: bool`, `column_widths: BTreeMap<String, f64>`, `resizing_column: Option<String>`, `virtual_scrolling: bool`, `total_rows: usize`, `total_cols: usize`, `loading: Bindable<bool>`. §1.2 Events: add `SetDirection(Direction)`, `ColumnResize`, `SetLoading(bool)`, `SetRowCounts`. §1.7 init: thread `dir` through. |

### Module layout (`table/mod.rs`)

Single file, ~1500–2000 LOC, organized in the same section order as
`tabs/mod.rs`:

1. `State` — single `Idle` variant per §1.1.1 (sort state is in Context).
2. `Event` — every event from §1.2 plus `SetDirection`, `ColumnResize`,
   `SetRowCounts`, `SetLoading`.
3. `EscapeKeyBehavior` — enum (ClearSelection / None) from §1.5.
4. `Messages` — five `MessageFn` fields (select_all, select_row,
   sort_ascending, sort_descending, sort_none); `ComponentMessages`
   marker; `Default` returns static-str fns.
5. `Props` — every field from §1.5 + `disallow_empty_selection`,
   `loading`, `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
   `dir: Direction`. `#[derive(ars_core::HasId)]`.
6. `Context` — every field from §1.3 plus the §6/§3.5/§1.6/spec-drift
   fields described above. Derives `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq` (not
   `Eq` — `MessageFn` only ptr-compares).
7. `Part` — `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "table"]` with all 14
   anatomy parts + `ColumnResizeHandle { column: String }`.
8. `Machine` — empty marker struct; impl `ars_core::Machine`. Uses
   `ars_core::NoEffect` for `type Effect` (no DOM effects — adapters
   read `ctx.focused_cell`/`ctx.focused_row`/`ctx.focused_col` and apply
   focus directly).
9. `Api<'a>` — every method from spec §1.8 plus the §6 resize-handle
   attrs and §3.5 virtual-scrolling additions to `table_attrs` and
   `row_attrs(row_id, index)`.

### Transitions (high-level)

Match `tabs::Machine::transition` style — each arm returns
`Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx| ...))` or `None` when
guarded out:

| Event | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `SortColumn { column }` | Toggle: None → Ascending → Descending → None. Update `ctx.sort_descriptor`, set `ctx.is_sorting = true`. |
| `SelectRow / DeselectRow / ToggleRow` | Reject when `selection_mode == None` or row in `disabled_keys`. Honor `disallow_empty_selection` on deselect: if removing the last selected row, return `None`. Delegate to `selection::State::{select, deselect, toggle}` and write `selected_keys` back to `Bindable`. |

| `RowAction(key)` | Reject when disabled. No mutation — adapter listens. |
| `SetDirection(dir)` | Idempotent. Replace `ctx.dir` only if new value differs. |
| `ColumnResize { column, width }` | Clamp to `min_column_width` (50px). Update `ctx.column_widths[column]` and set `ctx.resizing_column = Some(column)`. |
| `SetRowCounts { total_rows, total_cols }` | Replace `ctx.total_rows`/`ctx.total_cols`. |
| `SetLoading(bool)` | `ctx.loading.set(value)`. |

  `aria-valuenow=width`, `data-ars-resizing` when
  `ctx.resizing_column == Some(column)`.

  `ctx.virtual_scrolling`.

  `aria-rowindex` (1-based) when `ctx.virtual_scrolling`. Keep the
  existing `row_attrs(row_id)` for non-virtual callers as a thin
  delegate.
- `on_cell_keydown` resolves left/right against `ctx.dir == Direction::Rtl`
  exactly as written in spec §1.8.

### `ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatcher

Wire all 15 parts (§2.1 + §6 ColumnResizeHandle). Variants that need
extra data (`Part::ColumnHeader { header }`,
`Part::Cell { col, row }`, `Part::Row { key }`,
`Part::RowCheckbox { key }`, `Part::ExpandTrigger { key }`,
`Part::ExpandedContent { key }`, `Part::ColumnResizeHandle { column }`)
read those fields directly out of the variant.

`table/tests.rs` mirrors `tabs/tests.rs` layout:

| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_descending_after_second_click` | Cycle to descending. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_none_after_third_click` | Cycle clears. |
| `table_row_checkbox_aria_checked_false_default` | Unselected row. |
| `table_row_checkbox_aria_checked_true_after_toggle` | After `ToggleRow`. |
| `table_select_all_checkbox_mixed_when_partial` | `aria-checked="mixed"`. |
| `table_select_all_checkbox_true_when_all` | `aria-checked="true"`. |
| `table_select_all_event_only_when_multiple` | `SelectAll` is a no-op in `Mode::Single`. |

# Task #286 — Implement Table agnostic core

## Context

Task **#286** ("Implement Table agnostic core", 8 pts, complex tier) is the
framework-agnostic state machine for the `Table` data-display component. The
Collections Epic (#53) it depends on closed in 2026-04-08, so it is unblocked.

Spec: `spec/components/data-display/table.md` (1419 lines).

The Table is a stateful machine that owns row/column selection, sort
descriptors, expansion, grid focus, optional column-resize state, and
optional virtual-scrolling metadata. The agnostic core MUST NOT touch the
DOM — adapters apply focus, measure layout, and observe scroll. The core
produces ARIA + `data-ars-*` attributes via `ConnectApi` and the typed
`Api<'_>` so both Leptos and Dioxus adapters can render identical markup.

### Spec drift to fix in this task

The spec is internally inconsistent in three places that the implementation
must reconcile (per the CLAUDE.md rule "Resolve the spec/code mismatch in
the same task"):

1. **`Context::dir: Direction`** is missing from §1.3 but referenced by
   `Api::on_cell_keydown` (line 770) for RTL arrow-key resolution. Add the
   field and a `Direction::Auto → concrete` resolution in `init` via
   `env.locale`, plus a `SetDirection(Direction)` event so runtime
   direction changes are wired through.
2. **`Context::is_sorting: bool`** is described in §1.1.1 ("Visual
   Feedback") but absent from the §1.3 Context struct. Add it. Toggle it
   inside the `SortColumn` arm.
3. **`build_sort_announcement` (§3.4.3)** references undeclared `locale`
   and uses `SortDescriptor::column_key` instead of `column`. This is
   illustrative adapter code, not part of the agnostic core — leave the
   spec drift note for a follow-up spec-only PR (no impl impact here).

Items (1) and (2) are spec edits in the same PR; (3) is documented in the
PR body but not fixed.

## Approach

Implement the Table machine + connect API as a single Rust module living
in a `table/` directory next to existing data-display components, with
unit + snapshot tests inline via `mod tests;`. Use **Tabs** as the
structural template (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/`) — it is
the closest existing complex component (registered keys + selection +
keyboard nav + Bindable controlled/uncontrolled + Direction).

### Variants covered in this task

The issue's "Tests to add first" list names every variant explicitly:

- §5 SelectAll (mixed checkbox, `selection::Set::All`) → included
- §6 Column Resizing (`ColumnResizeHandle` part, `ColumnResize` event,
  `column_widths` + `resizing_column` in Context) → included
- §3.5 Virtual Scrolling (`virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
  `aria-rowcount`/`aria-colcount` + `aria-rowindex`) → included
- §1.6 Async data loading (`loading: Bindable<bool>`, minimal
  `SetLoading(bool)` event) → included (Bindable field + event; the
  `on_load_more` callback is adapter-only and is not stored in the core)
- §5 `BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper → **excluded**. The spec
  describes it as adapter/application code that wraps the canonical
  `selection::Set::All`; it is not part of the agnostic core.

### Files to create

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/mod.rs` | State, Event, Context, Props, Messages, Machine impl, Part enum, Api with all `*_attrs` and keyboard helpers |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/tests.rs` | Inline unit + snapshot tests for every named issue test |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/snapshots/` | insta `.snap` fixtures (auto-created) |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/data_display.rs` | Add `assert_anatomy` entry for Table (file may not yet exist — create) |

### Files to modify

| Path | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/mod.rs` | Add `pub mod table;` |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance.rs` | Register new `data_display` module if not present |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs` | Add `proptest_table_event_sequences_preserve_invariants` (selection ⊆ rows, expansion ⊆ rows, focus in bounds) |
| `spec/components/data-display/table.md` | §1.3 Context: add `dir: Direction`, `is_sorting: bool`, `column_widths: BTreeMap<String, f64>`, `resizing_column: Option<String>`, `virtual_scrolling: bool`, `total_rows: usize`, `total_cols: usize`, `loading: Bindable<bool>`. §1.2 Events: add `SetDirection(Direction)`, `ColumnResize`, `SetLoading(bool)`, `SetRowCounts`. §1.7 init: thread `dir` through. |

### Module layout (`table/mod.rs`)

Single file, ~1500–2000 LOC, organized in the same section order as
`tabs/mod.rs`:

1. `State` — single `Idle` variant per §1.1.1 (sort state is in Context).
2. `Event` — every event from §1.2 plus `SetDirection`, `ColumnResize`,
   `SetRowCounts`, `SetLoading`.
3. `EscapeKeyBehavior` — enum (ClearSelection / None) from §1.5.
4. `Messages` — five `MessageFn` fields (select_all, select_row,
   sort_ascending, sort_descending, sort_none); `ComponentMessages`
   marker; `Default` returns static-str fns.
5. `Props` — every field from §1.5 + `disallow_empty_selection`,
   `loading`, `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
   `dir: Direction`. `#[derive(ars_core::HasId)]`.
6. `Context` — every field from §1.3 plus the §6/§3.5/§1.6/spec-drift

7. `Part` — `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "table"]` with all 14

8. `Machine` — empty marker struct; impl `ars_core::Machine`. Uses
   `ars_core::NoEffect` for `type Effect` (no DOM effects — adapters

Context:
- Working dir: `/Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d`
- Spec: `spec/components/data-display/table.md` (1419 lines)
- Reference implementation: `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/{mod.rs, tests.rs}` (tabs is the closest complex component already shipped)
- Test fixtures pattern: `crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/snapshots/*.snap`
- Spec-conformance helper: `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/helper.rs`
- Proptest config: `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/common.rs`

What I want you to do: read my plan file, then read the spec file (`spec/components/data-display/table.md`) and the relevant existing reference files. Sanity-check the plan against:

1. **Spec coverage.** Does my plan capture every test the issue's "Tests to add first" lists? (`role=grid`/`table`, column headers w/ `aria-sort`, row selection, `SelectAll` w/ mixed, expandable rows w/ `aria-expanded`, column resize handles, keyboard nav across cells, sortable headers, sticky headers, empty state, row actions, `aria-rowcount`/`aria-colcount` for virtualized tables)? Did I miss any spec section that introduces a public API surface I should implement (e.g., the SelectAllMode enum from §5.1, the loading/on_load_more pattern from §1.6, the BulkSelection from §5.2, the `disabledBehavior` parity gap from §7)?

2. **Spec drift.** I identified three drifts I plan to fix in the same task (per the CLAUDE.md rule):
   (a) `Context::dir: Direction` missing from §1.3 but referenced by `on_cell_keydown` in §1.8.
   (b) `Context::is_sorting: bool` referenced in §1.1.1 "Visual Feedback" but absent from §1.3 Context.
   (c) `build_sort_announcement` in §3.4.3 uses undeclared `locale` and a `column_key` field that doesn't exist on SortDescriptor.

3. **Module / file layout.** My plan creates `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/{mod.rs, tests.rs, snapshots/}` (directory-backed per CLAUDE.md rule) and registers `pub mod table;` in the sibling `mod.rs`. Is this consistent with how `navigation::tabs` is shipped today? Should I split state.rs / api.rs / context.rs separately from mod.rs, or keep one big file (Tabs uses one ~2000-line file)?

4. **Variants in scope.** The "Tests to add first" list explicitly names column resize handles AND aria-rowcount/aria-colcount for virtualized tables, so I include §5 SelectAll, §6 Column Resizing, and §3.5 Virtual Scrolling in this PR. Is this the right call, or should any of those be split into separate follow-up issues to keep the diff reviewable? (Issue is 8 points already.)

5. **Effect type.** My plan uses `ars_core::NoEffect` for `type Effect`. Justification: every transition in spec §1.7 is `TransitionPlan::context_only(...)` — adapters read `ctx.focused_cell`/`ctx.focused_row` themselves and apply DOM focus without an effect dispatch. 6. **`disallow_empty_selection` behavior.** My plan rejects `DeselectRow` on the sole selected row and rejects `DeselectAll` when this flag is set. Is that consistent with how React Aria's `Table` behaves and with the rest of the workspace's selection conventions (e.g. Listbox, Menu — even though those aren't built yet)?

Examples I'm unsure about: how does `SelectAll` interact with `disallow_empty_selection`? What happens when a row is in both `disabled_keys` and the default selection? Does `RowAction` care about disabled state? How should `ColumnResize` behave when `column_widths` doesn't yet have an entry for that column?

Return a focused review (under 800 words) covering: (a) any missed spec requirements, (b) any spec drifts I missed, (c) plan-structure improvements, (d) variant-scope recommendation, (e) effect-type recommendation, (f) test-coverage gaps. Do not write code — your output is plan-level only.

content.text:
I have comprehensive context now. Here is my focused plan-level review.

---

# Review of Plan: Task #286 — Table agnostic core

## (a) Missed spec requirements

**M1. `EscapeKey` handling is not wired.** §1.5 defines `EscapeKeyBehavior::{ClearSelection, None}` and §1.7's transition table omits Escape entirely. The plan inherits this gap: there is no `Event::EscapeKey` (or equivalent), no transition arm, and no test. Because `disallow_empty_selection` is one of the named tests and *its closest sibling* in Tabs is also driven by the Escape interaction, leaving Escape unimplemented in this PR creates the same "feature wired in spec but not in code" drift that you are explicitly trying to avoid. Add `Event::EscapeKey` plus a transition arm that calls `selection::State::clear` when `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection`, and add a test `table_escape_clears_selection_when_clear_selection`.

**M2. `min_column_width` and `column_resize_step` props are missing.** §6.4 names both (`min_column_width: 50px`, `step: 10px`). The plan only mentions the 50px constant inside the transition. Promote both to `Props` fields so the clamp is configurable, and so keyboard width adjustments have an addressable step. Otherwise §6.4's keyboard contract ("`ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight` adjust width by `step`") has nowhere to source the step from — and there is no `on_resize_handle_keydown` API method in the plan.

**M3. `column_resize_handle_attrs` does not emit `data-ars-part` / `data-ars-scope`.** The plan lists role/orientation/valuenow but skips the standard scope+part block that every other anatomy part emits in spec §1.7. Trivial, but the spec-conformance helper checks part names round-trip.

**M4. `SelectAllMode` enum (§5.1) is absent from the plan.** The plan says "BulkSelection is excluded as adapter-only," which is fine, but `SelectAllMode::{None, AllVisible, AllData { total_count }}` is *not* adapter-only — it controls whether `select_all_attrs` is even produced and the `aria-label` it carries ("Select all 1,204 items"). Add it as a `Props` field plus an `Api::select_all_visible()` helper, OR document explicitly in the PR that you are deferring the AllData label variant for a follow-up and confirm the conformance test still passes without it.

**M5. `aria-controls`, `aria-labelledby`, and ID derivation are partly missing for §3.5.** The `aria-rowindex` test exists but there is no test for the `aria-colindex` analog (some adapters emit it for cells when virtualized). §3.5 itself only requires rowcount/colcount/rowindex, so this is optional, but worth noting in the audit checklist.

**M6. `ColumnHeader` test for non-sortable columns is missing.** The plan tests aria-sort for sortable headers, but §3.4.2 explicitly requires "Non-sortable columns MUST NOT include `aria-sort`." Add `table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort`.

## (b) Spec drifts you didn't list

**D1. §1.1.1 mentions `Idle`, `Scrolling`, and other interaction states**, but §1.1 lists only `Idle`. Either remove the misleading `Scrolling` reference in §1.1.1 *or* add a `Scrolling` state. Since the plan correctly keeps State as a single `Idle` variant, the spec edit is the right resolution.

**D2. §1.7 `Event::Focus { cell }` writes `ctx.focused_cell = Some(c)`, but `Event::FocusCell { row, col }` writes `ctx.focused_cell = Some((c, 0))` — hard-coding row 0!** This is a bug in the spec code, not the plan, but you will hit it on a copy-paste pass. Resolve by having `FocusCell` resolve the row index from `all_row_ids` (passed via an event payload or computed in adapter). Cleanest fix: change `Event::FocusCell` to `FocusCell { cell: (usize, usize), row: Key }` so both indices are explicit. Document this in the PR body as drift item (4).

**D3. §1.8 `on_cell_keydown` references `ctx.dir` which doesn't exist (your drift item (a)) and also accepts `col_count` as a parameter while having no API for `Ctrl+Home` / `Ctrl+End` from §3.2.** The plan tests `Home` but not `Ctrl+Home` (jump to first cell of first row). Add a test or drop the `Ctrl+Home` requirement from §3.2 — pick one.

**D4. `Event::FocusRow` and `Event::FocusCell` exist in §1.7 but are not listed in §1.2 events table.** Add them to §1.2 in the same PR (consistent with how you handle the Direction drift).

**D5. §3.4.3's `build_sort_announcement` you flagged as illustrative — but the spec file lives in *the spec corpus that gets validated by `cargo xtask spec validate`*. Recommend you delete the broken code block entirely or move it to a fenced-out commentary block in the same PR, rather than deferring; otherwise spec lint may flag it.

## (c) Plan-structure improvements

**Layout decision: keep one `mod.rs` like Tabs.** The Tabs precedent is unambiguous — one 1998-line `mod.rs` + tests.rs + snapshots/. The plan correctly follows this. Do *not* split into `state.rs` / `api.rs` / `context.rs`. The workspace convention is the directory-backed module with a single mod.rs *only when there are child modules*; here there are none, so technically a flat `table.rs` would also be valid — but the directory form is needed because of the `snapshots/` sibling, so the plan is right.

**Add a `SyncProps` event.** Tabs has it (re-applies disabled-key invariants when props change). Without it, runtime changes to `disabled_keys`/`selection_mode`/`disallow_empty_selection` cannot reach the machine. The plan currently has `SetDirection`, `ColumnResize`, `SetRowCounts`, `SetLoading` but no general SyncProps. Add one, mirroring Tabs §1.7.

**Add `SyncControlledValue` analogs** for each of the three Bindable fields (`selected_rows`, `expanded_rows`, `sort_descriptor`). Tabs has `SyncControlledValue(Option<Key>)`; Table needs three because it has three controlled fields. This is what enables controlled-mode parent updates to flow through. Currently the plan has none — adapters cannot honor `<Table value={signal} />` re-renders.

## (d) Variant-scope recommendation

**Trim §3.5 Virtual Scrolling from the PR.** The "Tests to add first" list names it, but the virtualizer integration (§3.5 with its prose about `Virtualizer`, `IntersectionObserver`, sentinel placement) is a substantial sub-feature whose state belongs in `ars-collections::Virtualizer`, not in this machine. What you *can* and should include is the ARIA-only slice: `aria-rowcount`/`aria-colcount`/`aria-rowindex` driven by three flat Context fields (`virtual_scrolling: bool`, `total_rows: usize`, `total_cols: usize`). Your plan already does this — just label it clearly in the PR as "ARIA-only virtual scroll surface; `ScrollToRow` event + `Virtualizer` integration deferred to a follow-up issue."

**Keep §5 SelectAll and §6 Column Resizing in the PR.** Both are named in the tests-first list and both are small. Do *not* split out.

**Defer `on_load_more` callback wiring.** The plan correctly keeps `loading: Bindable<bool>` in the core and explicitly notes `on_load_more` is adapter-only. Good call — `Callback<()>` is an adapter type. Add a sentence to the PR body confirming this.

## (e) Effect type recommendation

**Keep `NoEffect` — your justification is correct.** Tabs emits `Effect::FocusFocusedTab` because Tabs *implicitly* moves DOM focus on `SelectTab` (the roving-tabindex pattern). Table is different: the agnostic core only manipulates `focused_cell`/`focused_row`/`focused_col` and the adapter reads those plus the `tabindex` from `cell_attrs` to decide what to focus on the next render. There is no implicit programmatic-focus moment, so no effect is needed.

**One nuance:** `Event::ScrollToRow(Key)` from §3.5 (if you ever add it) *would* need an effect — `Effect::ScrollRowIntoView { row }`. Since you are deferring that event per (d), `NoEffect` is correct *for this PR*. Note in the PR body that adding ScrollToRow later will require widening the Effect enum.

## (f) Test-coverage gaps

**Add these to the named-test list:**

1. **`table_select_all_respects_disallow_empty_selection`** — selecting then deselecting all when flag is set should fail to clear the last row.
2. **`table_select_all_event_only_when_multiple`** is named — also add `table_deselect_all_no_op_in_mode_none`.
3. **`table_disabled_default_selection_is_pruned_at_init`** — when a key appears in both `disabled_keys` and `default_selected_rows`, selection should not include it. (This is the case you raised. Spec is silent — implementer's choice; recommend pruning at init and adding to spec §1.7 init.)
4. **`table_row_action_disabled_returns_none`** is named — also add `table_row_action_with_selection_mode_none_passes_through` to lock in that RowAction is independent of selection.
5. **`table_column_resize_unknown_column_creates_entry`** — your question about `column_widths` missing entries. Recommend "first resize creates the entry," matching the `BTreeMap::insert` semantics; spec is silent so document the choice.
6. **`table_column_resize_clamps_to_min_width`** is named — also add `table_column_resize_below_min_width_via_step` and `table_column_resize_does_not_track_resizing_column_after_release` (requires a `ColumnResizeEnd` event you don't have — add it, or document that `resizing_column` is cleared by `pointerup` in the adapter).
7. **`table_set_direction_idempotent`** — your transition table says idempotent; needs a test.
8. **`table_set_loading_suppresses_via_bindable`** — confirms the Bindable round-trip.
9. **`table_focus_cell_then_blur_clears_all_three_focus_fields`** — spec contract per §1.7.
10. **`table_set_tabs`-equivalent registration test.** Critical question: how does the machine know which rows exist? Tabs uses `Event::SetTabs(Vec<TabRegistration>)`. Table's `on_row_keydown` takes `all_row_ids: &[&Key]` as a parameter rather than registering — that means the machine has no row registry, which means `disallow_empty_selection` cannot validate against the current row set. Either register rows via `Event::SetRows` (mirror Tabs) or document explicitly that all row-membership logic relies on adapter-supplied parameters per call.

## Critical Files for Implementation

- /Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d/spec/components/data-display/table.md
- /Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d/crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/mod.rs
- /Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d/crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/tests.rs
- /Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d/crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/helper.rs
- /Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d/crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs

4. **`table_row_action_disabled_returns_none`** is named — also add `table_row_action_with_selection_mode_none_passes_through` to lock in that RowAction is independent of selection.
5. **`table_column_resize_unknown_column_creates_entry`** — your question about `column_widths` missing entries. Recommend "first resize creates the entry," matching the `BTreeMap::insert` semantics; spec is silent so document the choice.
6. **`table_column_resize_clamps_to_min_width`** is named — also add `table_column_resize_below_min_width_via_step` and `table_column_resize_does_not_track_resizing_column_after_release` (requires a `ColumnResizeEnd` event you don't have — add it, or document that `resizing_column` is cleared by `pointerup` in the adapter).
7. **`table_set_direction_idempotent`** — your transition table says idempotent; needs a test.
8. **`table_set_loading_suppresses_via_bindable`** — confirms the Bindable round-trip.
9. **`table_focus_cell_then_blur_clears_all_three_focus_fields`** — spec contract per §1.7.
10. **`table_set_tabs`-equivalent registration test.** Critical question: how does the machine know which rows exist? Tabs uses `Event::SetTabs(Vec<TabRegistration>)`. Table's `on_row_keydown` takes `all_row_ids: &[&Key]` as a parameter rather than registering — that means the machine has no row registry, which means `disallow_empty_selection` cannot validate against the current row set. Either register rows via `Event::SetRows` (mirror Tabs) or document explicitly that all row-membership logic relies on adapter-supplied parameters per call.

input.old_string:
### Spec drift to fix in this task

The spec is internally inconsistent in three places that the implementation
must reconcile (per the CLAUDE.md rule "Resolve the spec/code mismatch in
the same task"):

1. **`Context::dir: Direction`** is missing from §1.3 but referenced by
   `Api::on_cell_keydown` (line 770) for RTL arrow-key resolution. Add the
   field and a `Direction::Auto → concrete` resolution in `init` via
   `env.locale`, plus a `SetDirection(Direction)` event so runtime
   direction changes are wired through.
2. **`Context::is_sorting: bool`** is described in §1.1.1 ("Visual
   Feedback") but absent from the §1.3 Context struct. Add it. Toggle it
   inside the `SortColumn` arm.
3. **`build_sort_announcement` (§3.4.3)** references undeclared `locale`
   and uses `SortDescriptor::column_key` instead of `column`. This is
   illustrative adapter code, not part of the agnostic core — leave the
   spec drift note for a follow-up spec-only PR (no impl impact here).

Items (1) and (2) are spec edits in the same PR; (3) is documented in the
PR body but not fixed.
input.new_string:
### Spec drift to fix in this task

The spec is internally inconsistent in several places. Per the CLAUDE.md
rule "Resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task," every drift below
is fixed in this PR — the spec edits land alongside the code:

1. **`Context::dir: Direction`** missing from §1.3 but referenced by
   `Api::on_cell_keydown` for RTL arrow-key resolution. Add the field and
   resolve `Direction::Auto → concrete` in `init` from `env.locale`. Add
   `Event::SetDirection(Direction)` so runtime changes are wired through.
2. **`Context::is_sorting: bool`** described in §1.1.1 but absent from
   §1.3. Add it; toggle inside the `SortColumn` arm.
3. **§1.1.1 mentions `Scrolling` state** but §1.1 lists only `Idle`.
   Resolve by deleting the misleading reference in the spec (the
   single-`Idle` design is correct — sort lives in Context, scroll lives
   in adapter).
4. **§1.7 `Event::FocusCell` writes `Some((c, 0))`** — a literal bug that
   hard-codes row 0. Fix the spec **and** code: `Event::FocusCell` carries
   `{ row: Key, col: usize, row_index: usize }`, and the transition writes
   `(col, row_index)` to `focused_cell`.
5. **§1.2 events table omits `FocusRow` and `FocusCell`** even though
   §1.7 defines them. Add both rows to §1.2.
6. **§3.4.3 `build_sort_announcement`** references undeclared `locale` and
   uses `SortDescriptor::column_key` (the real field is `column`). Rewrite
   that code block in the spec to compile against the real `SortDescriptor`
   API. (Adapter helper, not in the agnostic core — but it must compile
   under `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets`.)

- §5 SelectAll (mixed checkbox, `selection::Set::All`) → included
- §6 Column Resizing (`ColumnResizeHandle` part, `ColumnResize` event,
  `column_widths` + `resizing_column` in Context) → included
- §3.5 Virtual Scrolling (`virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
  `aria-rowcount`/`aria-colcount` + `aria-rowindex`) → included
- §1.6 Async data loading (`loading: Bindable<bool>`, minimal
