input.new_string:
### Variants covered in this task

The issue's "Tests to add first" list names every variant explicitly:

- §5 SelectAll (mixed checkbox, `selection::Set::All`, **plus
  `SelectAllMode::{None, AllVisible, AllData { total_count }}` as a Props
  field** because it controls whether `SelectAllCheckbox` is rendered and
  what `aria-label` it carries) → included
- §6 Column Resizing (`ColumnResizeHandle` part, `ColumnResize` and
  `ColumnResizeEnd` events, `column_widths` + `resizing_column` in
  Context, **`min_column_width: f64` and `column_resize_step: f64`
  Props**, **`on_resize_handle_keydown` Api method** for keyboard width
  adjustments per §6.4) → included
- §3.5 Virtual Scrolling **(ARIA-only slice in this PR)**:
  `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols` Context fields →
  `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` on the table, `aria-rowindex` on
  rows. The full `Virtualizer` integration and the `ScrollToRow(Key)`
  event are explicitly **deferred** to a follow-up issue — they would
  require widening `Effect` from `NoEffect` to a typed enum and pulling
  in `ars-collections::Virtualizer` plumbing.
- §1.6 Async data loading (`loading: Bindable<bool>`, minimal
  `SetLoading(bool)` event) → included (Bindable field + event). The
  `on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>>` callback is **adapter-only** and
  is not stored in the core.
- §5 `BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper → **excluded**. The spec
  describes it as adapter/application code that wraps the canonical
  `selection::Set::All`; it is not part of the agnostic core.

### Row registry

The machine needs to know the full row list to validate
`disallow_empty_selection`, derive `aria-rowcount` semantics, and prune
`default_selected_rows` against `disabled_keys` at init. Mirror Tabs'
`Event::SetTabs(Vec<TabRegistration>)`:

- Add **`Event::SetRows(Vec<Key>)`** — replaces `ctx.rows` and snaps
  selection / expansion to the new set.
- Add `Context::rows: Vec<Key>`. The keyboard helpers
  (`on_row_keydown`, `on_cell_keydown`) keep their `all_row_ids: &[&Key]`
  parameter (adapter slice for that render) — the registry exists for
  invariants, not for navigation walks.
- Init prunes any key that appears in both `disabled_keys` and
  `default_selected_rows` from the seed selection (workspace convention;
  spec is silent — document the choice inline).

### Prop sync (controlled-mode parent updates)

Tabs has `Event::SyncProps` plus `Event::SyncControlledValue`. Table has
three Bindable fields, so the plan uses one coalesced event per Bindable
plus a general `SyncProps` for non-Bindable runtime changes:

- **`Event::SyncProps`** — re-applies `selection_mode`,
  `selection_behavior`, `disabled_keys`, `disallow_empty_selection`,
  `escape_key_behavior`, `interactive`, `sticky_header`,
  `min_column_width`, `column_resize_step` from props. Re-runs the
  selection invariant.
- **`Event::SyncControlledSelectedRows(Option<selection::Set>)`**
- **`Event::SyncControlledExpandedRows(Option<BTreeSet<Key>>)`**
- **`Event::SyncControlledSortDescriptor(Option<SortDescriptor<String>>)`**

Each `SyncControlled*` calls `Bindable::sync_controlled` on the matching
Context field.

### Escape-key handling

§1.5 names `EscapeKeyBehavior::{ClearSelection, None}`. The transition
table in §1.7 omits it — add:

- **`Event::EscapeKey`** — when `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection`
  and the current selection is non-empty, dispatch the same context
  mutation as `DeselectAll` (subject to `disallow_empty_selection`).
  Otherwise no transition.

input.old_string:
| `SetDirection(dir)` | Idempotent. Replace `ctx.dir` only if new value differs. |
| `ColumnResize { column, width }` | Clamp to `min_column_width` (50px). Update `ctx.column_widths[column]` and set `ctx.resizing_column = Some(column)`. |
| `SetRowCounts { total_rows, total_cols }` | Replace `ctx.total_rows`/`ctx.total_cols`. |
| `SetLoading(bool)` | `ctx.loading.set(value)`. |
input.new_string:
| `SetDirection(dir)` | Idempotent. Replace `ctx.dir` only if new value differs. |
| `SetRows(rows)` | Replace `ctx.rows`. Prune `selected_rows` / `expanded_rows` of keys not present. Clear `focused_row`/`focused_cell` if pointing at a removed row. |
| `EscapeKey` | When `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection` and selection is non-empty, clear (subject to `disallow_empty_selection`). |
| `ColumnResize { column, width }` | Clamp to `props.min_column_width` (default 50). Upsert `ctx.column_widths[column]` (create-on-first-resize) and set `ctx.resizing_column = Some(column)`. |
| `ColumnResizeEnd { column }` | Clear `ctx.resizing_column` when it matches `column`. |
| `SetRowCounts { total_rows, total_cols }` | Replace `ctx.total_rows`/`ctx.total_cols`. |
| `SetLoading(bool)` | `ctx.loading.set(value)`. |
| `SyncProps` | Re-apply non-Bindable Props fields; re-prune selection/expansion against new `disabled_keys`. |
| `SyncControlledSelectedRows(opt)` | `ctx.selected_rows.sync_controlled(opt)`. |
| `SyncControlledExpandedRows(opt)` | `ctx.expanded_rows.sync_controlled(opt)`. |
| `SyncControlledSortDescriptor(opt)` | `ctx.sort_descriptor.sync_controlled(opt)`. |

input.old_string:
### Api / attribute methods

Every method from §1.8 verbatim, plus:

- `column_resize_handle_attrs(column: &str, current_width: f64) -> AttrMap`
  — emits `role="separator"`, `aria-orientation="vertical"`,
  `aria-valuenow=width`, `data-ars-resizing` when
  `ctx.resizing_column == Some(column)`.
- `table_attrs` — adds `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` when
  `ctx.virtual_scrolling`.
- A virtual-scrolling-aware `row_attrs(row_id, index)` that emits
  `aria-rowindex` (1-based) when `ctx.virtual_scrolling`. Keep the
  existing `row_attrs(row_id)` for non-virtual callers as a thin
  delegate.
- `on_cell_keydown` resolves left/right against `ctx.dir == Direction::Rtl`
  exactly as written in spec §1.8.
input.new_string:
### Api / attribute methods

Every method from §1.8 verbatim, plus:

- `column_resize_handle_attrs(column: &str, current_width: f64) -> AttrMap`
  — emits the standard `data-ars-scope="table"` / `data-ars-part=
  "column-resize-handle"`, plus `role="separator"`,
  `aria-orientation="vertical"`, `aria-valuenow=width`, and
  `data-ars-resizing` when `ctx.resizing_column == Some(column)`.
- `on_resize_handle_keydown(column: &str, current_width: f64,
  data: &KeyboardEventData)` — emits `Event::ColumnResize` with
  `width ± ctx.column_resize_step` on `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight`,
  RTL-aware via `ctx.dir`.
- `table_attrs` — adds `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` when
  `ctx.virtual_scrolling`.
- A virtual-scrolling-aware `row_attrs(row_id, row_index: usize)` that
  emits `aria-rowindex` (1-based) when `ctx.virtual_scrolling`. The
  non-virtualized `row_attrs(row_id)` from §1.8 is kept as a thin
  delegate that passes `row_index = 0`.
- `select_all_attrs` short-circuits to an empty `AttrMap` when
  `props.select_all_mode == SelectAllMode::None`, and substitutes the
  `AllData { total_count }` count into the `aria-label` via
  `Messages::select_all`.
- `column_header_attrs(column, sortable=false)` MUST omit `aria-sort`
  entirely (§3.4.2). The spec's example sets `aria-sort="none"`
  unconditionally — that is wrong; the named test
  `table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort` locks the correct
  behavior.
- `on_cell_keydown` resolves left/right against `ctx.dir == Direction::Rtl`,
  handles `Ctrl+Home`/`Ctrl+End` per §3.2 in addition to the §1.8
  Home/End/Arrow set.

input.plan:
# Task #286 — Implement Table agnostic core

## Context

Task **#286** ("Implement Table agnostic core", 8 pts, complex tier) is the
framework-agnostic state machine for the `Table` data-display component. The
Collections Epic (#53) it depends on closed in 2026-04-08, so it is unblocked.

Spec: `spec/components/data-display/table.md` (1419 lines).

The Table is a stateful machine that owns row/column selection, sort
descriptors, expansion, grid focus, optional column-resize state, and
optional virtual-scrolling metadata. The agnostic core MUST NOT touch the
DOM — adapters apply focus, measure layout, and observe scroll. The core
produces ARIA + `data-ars-*` attributes via `ConnectApi` and the typed
`Api<'_>` so both Leptos and Dioxus adapters can render identical markup.

### Spec drift to fix in this task

The spec is internally inconsistent in several places. Per the CLAUDE.md
rule "Resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task," every drift below
is fixed in this PR — the spec edits land alongside the code:

1. **`Context::dir: Direction`** missing from §1.3 but referenced by
   `Api::on_cell_keydown` for RTL arrow-key resolution. Add the field and
   resolve `Direction::Auto → concrete` in `init` from `env.locale`. Add
   `Event::SetDirection(Direction)` so runtime changes are wired through.
2. **`Context::is_sorting: bool`** described in §1.1.1 but absent from
   §1.3. Add it; toggle inside the `SortColumn` arm.
3. **§1.1.1 mentions `Scrolling` state** but §1.1 lists only `Idle`.
   Resolve by deleting the misleading reference in the spec (the
   single-`Idle` design is correct — sort lives in Context, scroll lives
   in adapter).
4. **§1.7 `Event::FocusCell` writes `Some((c, 0))`** — a literal bug that
   hard-codes row 0. Fix the spec **and** code: `Event::FocusCell` carries
   `{ row: Key, col: usize, row_index: usize }`, and the transition writes
   `(col, row_index)` to `focused_cell`.
5. **§1.2 events table omits `FocusRow` and `FocusCell`** even though
   §1.7 defines them. Add both rows to §1.2.
6. **§3.4.3 `build_sort_announcement`** references undeclared `locale` and
   uses `SortDescriptor::column_key` (the real field is `column`). Rewrite
   that code block in the spec to compile against the real `SortDescriptor`
   API. (Adapter helper, not in the agnostic core — but it must compile
   under `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets`.)

## Approach

Implement the Table machine + connect API as a single Rust module living
in a `table/` directory next to existing data-display components, with
unit + snapshot tests inline via `mod tests;`. Use **Tabs** as the
structural template (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/`) — it is
the closest existing complex component (registered keys + selection +
keyboard nav + Bindable controlled/uncontrolled + Direction).

### Variants covered in this task

The issue's "Tests to add first" list names every variant explicitly:

- §5 SelectAll (mixed checkbox, `selection::Set::All`, **plus
  `SelectAllMode::{None, AllVisible, AllData { total_count }}` as a Props
  field** because it controls whether `SelectAllCheckbox` is rendered and
  what `aria-label` it carries) → included
- §6 Column Resizing (`ColumnResizeHandle` part, `ColumnResize` and
  `ColumnResizeEnd` events, `column_widths` + `resizing_column` in
  Context, **`min_column_width: f64` and `column_resize_step: f64`
  Props**, **`on_resize_handle_keydown` Api method** for keyboard width
  adjustments per §6.4) → included
- §3.5 Virtual Scrolling **(ARIA-only slice in this PR)**:
  `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols` Context fields →
  `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` on the table, `aria-rowindex` on
  rows. The full `Virtualizer` integration and the `ScrollToRow(Key)`
  event are explicitly **deferred** to a follow-up issue — they would
  require widening `Effect` from `NoEffect` to a typed enum and pulling
  in `ars-collections::Virtualizer` plumbing.
- §1.6 Async data loading (`loading: Bindable<bool>`, minimal
  `SetLoading(bool)` event) → included (Bindable field + event). The
  `on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>>` callback is **adapter-only** and
  is not stored in the core.
- §5 `BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper → **excluded**. The spec
  describes it as adapter/application code that wraps the canonical
  `selection::Set::All`; it is not part of the agnostic core.

### Row registry

The machine needs to know the full row list to validate
`disallow_empty_selection`, derive `aria-rowcount` semantics, and prune
`default_selected_rows` against `disabled_keys` at init. Mirror Tabs'
`Event::SetTabs(Vec<TabRegistration>)`:

- Add **`Event::SetRows(Vec<Key>)`** — replaces `ctx.rows` and snaps
  selection / expansion to the new set.
- Add `Context::rows: Vec<Key>`. The keyboard helpers
  (`on_row_keydown`, `on_cell_keydown`) keep their `all_row_ids: &[&Key]`
  parameter (adapter slice for that render) — the registry exists for
  invariants, not for navigation walks.
- Init prunes any key that appears in both `disabled_keys` and
  `default_selected_rows` from the seed selection (workspace convention;
  spec is silent — document the choice inline).

### Prop sync (controlled-mode parent updates)

Tabs has `Event::SyncProps` plus `Event::SyncControlledValue`. Table has
three Bindable fields, so the plan uses one coalesced event per Bindable
plus a general `SyncProps` for non-Bindable runtime changes:

- **`Event::SyncProps`** — re-applies `selection_mode`,
  `selection_behavior`, `disabled_keys`, `disallow_empty_selection`,
  `escape_key_behavior`, `interactive`, `sticky_header`,
  `min_column_width`, `column_resize_step` from props. Re-runs the
  selection invariant.
- **`Event::SyncControlledSelectedRows(Option<selection::Set>)`**
- **`Event::SyncControlledExpandedRows(Option<BTreeSet<Key>>)`**
- **`Event::SyncControlledSortDescriptor(Option<SortDescriptor<String>>)`**

Each `SyncControlled*` calls `Bindable::sync_controlled` on the matching
Context field.

### Escape-key handling

§1.5 names `EscapeKeyBehavior::{ClearSelection, None}`. The transition
table in §1.7 omits it — add:

- **`Event::EscapeKey`** — when `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection`
  and the current selection is non-empty, dispatch the same context
  mutation as `DeselectAll` (subject to `disallow_empty_selection`).
  Otherwise no transition.

### Files to create

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/mod.rs` | State, Event, Context, Props, Messages, Machine impl, Part enum, Api with all `*_attrs` and keyboard helpers |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/tests.rs` | Inline unit + snapshot tests for every named issue test |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/snapshots/` | insta `.snap` fixtures (auto-created) |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/data_display.rs` | Add `assert_anatomy` entry for Table (file may not yet exist — create) |

### Files to modify

| Path | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/mod.rs` | Add `pub mod table;` |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance.rs` | Register new `data_display` module if not present |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs` | Add `proptest_table_event_sequences_preserve_invariants` (selection ⊆ rows, expansion ⊆ rows, focus in bounds) |
| `spec/components/data-display/table.md` | §1.3 Context: add `dir: Direction`, `is_sorting: bool`, `column_widths: BTreeMap<String, f64>`, `resizing_column: Option<String>`, `virtual_scrolling: bool`, `total_rows: usize`, `total_cols: usize`, `loading: Bindable<bool>`. §1.2 Events: add `SetDirection(Direction)`, `ColumnResize`, `SetLoading(bool)`, `SetRowCounts`. §1.7 init: thread `dir` through. |

### Module layout (`table/mod.rs`)

Single file, ~1500–2000 LOC, organized in the same section order as
`tabs/mod.rs`:

1. `State` — single `Idle` variant per §1.1.1 (sort state is in Context).
2. `Event` — every event from §1.2 plus `SetDirection`, `ColumnResize`,
   `SetRowCounts`, `SetLoading`.
3. `EscapeKeyBehavior` — enum (ClearSelection / None) from §1.5.
4. `Messages` — five `MessageFn` fields (select_all, select_row,
   sort_ascending, sort_descending, sort_none); `ComponentMessages`
   marker; `Default` returns static-str fns.
5. `Props` — every field from §1.5 + `disallow_empty_selection`,
   `loading`, `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
   `dir: Direction`. `#[derive(ars_core::HasId)]`.
6. `Context` — every field from §1.3 plus the §6/§3.5/§1.6/spec-drift
   fields described above. Derives `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq` (not
   `Eq` — `MessageFn` only ptr-compares).
7. `Part` — `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "table"]` with all 14
   anatomy parts + `ColumnResizeHandle { column: String }`.
8. `Machine` — empty marker struct; impl `ars_core::Machine`. Uses
   `ars_core::NoEffect` for `type Effect` (no DOM effects — adapters
   read `ctx.focused_cell`/`ctx.focused_row`/`ctx.focused_col` and apply
   focus directly).
9. `Api<'a>` — every method from spec §1.8 plus the §6 resize-handle
   attrs and §3.5 virtual-scrolling additions to `table_attrs` and
   `row_attrs(row_id, index)`.

### Transitions (high-level)

Match `tabs::Machine::transition` style — each arm returns
`Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx| ...))` or `None` when
guarded out:

| Event | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `SortColumn { column }` | Toggle: None → Ascending → Descending → None. Update `ctx.sort_descriptor`, set `ctx.is_sorting = true`. |
| `SelectRow / DeselectRow / ToggleRow` | Reject when `selection_mode == None` or row in `disabled_keys`. Honor `disallow_empty_selection` on deselect: if removing the last selected row, return `None`. Delegate to `selection::State::{select, deselect, toggle}` and write `selected_keys` back to `Bindable`. |
| `SelectAll` | Reject unless `Mode::Multiple`. Call `selection::State::select_all()`. |
| `DeselectAll` | Reject when `Mode::None`. Reject when `disallow_empty_selection` and selection is non-empty (return `None` to preserve last). |
| `ExpandRow / CollapseRow` | Skip when no-op (already in/out of set). Mutate cloned `BTreeSet<Key>`. |
| `Focus { cell }` / `FocusRow` / `FocusCell` | Reject when `!ctx.interactive`. Update `focused_cell`/`focused_row`/`focused_col`. |
| `Blur` | Always: clear all three focus fields. |
| `RowAction(key)` | Reject when disabled. No mutation — adapter listens. |
| `SetDirection(dir)` | Idempotent. Replace `ctx.dir` only if new value differs. |
| `SetRows(rows)` | Replace `ctx.rows`. Prune `selected_rows` / `expanded_rows` of keys not present. Clear `focused_row`/`focused_cell` if pointing at a removed row. |
| `EscapeKey` | When `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection` and selection is non-empty, clear (subject to `disallow_empty_selection`). |
| `ColumnResize { column, width }` | Clamp to `props.min_column_width` (default 50). Upsert `ctx.column_widths[column]` (create-on-first-resize) and set `ctx.resizing_column = Some(column)`. |
| `ColumnResizeEnd { column }` | Clear `ctx.resizing_column` when it matches `column`. |
| `SetRowCounts { total_rows, total_cols }` | Replace `ctx.total_rows`/`ctx.total_cols`. |
| `SetLoading(bool)` | `ctx.loading.set(value)`. |
| `SyncProps` | Re-apply non-Bindable Props fields; re-prune selection/expansion against new `disabled_keys`. |
| `SyncControlledSelectedRows(opt)` | `ctx.selected_rows.sync_controlled(opt)`. |
| `SyncControlledExpandedRows(opt)` | `ctx.expanded_rows.sync_controlled(opt)`. |
| `SyncControlledSortDescriptor(opt)` | `ctx.sort_descriptor.sync_controlled(opt)`. |

### Api / attribute methods

Every method from §1.8 verbatim, plus:

- `column_resize_handle_attrs(column: &str, current_width: f64) -> AttrMap`
  — emits the standard `data-ars-scope="table"` / `data-ars-part=
  "column-resize-handle"`, plus `role="separator"`,
  `aria-orientation="vertical"`, `aria-valuenow=width`, and
  `data-ars-resizing` when `ctx.resizing_column == Some(column)`.
- `on_resize_handle_keydown(column: &str, current_width: f64,
  data: &KeyboardEventData)` — emits `Event::ColumnResize` with
  `width ± ctx.column_resize_step` on `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight`,
  RTL-aware via `ctx.dir`.
- `table_attrs` — adds `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` when
  `ctx.virtual_scrolling`.
- A virtual-scrolling-aware `row_attrs(row_id, row_index: usize)` that
  emits `aria-rowindex` (1-based) when `ctx.virtual_scrolling`. The
  non-virtualized `row_attrs(row_id)` from §1.8 is kept as a thin
  delegate that passes `row_index = 0`.
- `select_all_attrs` short-circuits to an empty `AttrMap` when
  `props.select_all_mode == SelectAllMode::None`, and substitutes the
  `AllData { total_count }` count into the `aria-label` via
  `Messages::select_all`.
- `column_header_attrs(column, sortable=false)` MUST omit `aria-sort`
  entirely (§3.4.2). The spec's example sets `aria-sort="none"`
  unconditionally — that is wrong; the named test
  `table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort` locks the correct
  behavior.
- `on_cell_keydown` resolves left/right against `ctx.dir == Direction::Rtl`,
  handles `Ctrl+Home`/`Ctrl+End` per §3.2 in addition to the §1.8
  Home/End/Arrow set.

### `ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatcher

Wire all 15 parts (§2.1 + §6 ColumnResizeHandle). Variants that need
extra data (`Part::ColumnHeader { header }`,
`Part::Cell { col, row }`, `Part::Row { key }`,
`Part::RowCheckbox { key }`, `Part::ExpandTrigger { key }`,
`Part::ExpandedContent { key }`, `Part::ColumnResizeHandle { column }`)
read those fields directly out of the variant.

### Tests (named in issue)

`table/tests.rs` mirrors `tabs/tests.rs` layout:

| Test | Asserts |
|------|---------|
| `table_root_produces_table_role` | `table_attrs()` has `role="table"` when `interactive=false`. |
| `table_root_produces_grid_role` | `role="grid"` when `interactive=true`. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_none_default` | New sortable column: `aria-sort="none"`. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_ascending_after_click` | After `SortColumn { column }`: `aria-sort="ascending"`. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_descending_after_second_click` | Cycle to descending. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_none_after_third_click` | Cycle clears. |
| `table_row_checkbox_aria_checked_false_default` | Unselected row. |
| `table_row_checkbox_aria_checked_true_after_toggle` | After `ToggleRow`. |
| `table_select_all_checkbox_mixed_when_partial` | `aria-checked="mixed"`. |
| `table_select_all_checkbox_true_when_all` | `aria-checked="true"`. |
| `table_select_all_event_only_when_multiple` | `SelectAll` is a no-op in `Mode::Single`. |
| `table_expand_trigger_aria_expanded_after_event` | `aria-expanded="true"` + `data-ars-expanded`. |
| `table_expanded_content_hidden_when_collapsed` | `hidden` attr present when collapsed. |
| `table_column_resize_handle_role_separator` | `role="separator"` + `aria-orientation="vertical"`. |
| `table_column_resize_handle_aria_valuenow_after_resize` | `aria-valuenow` reflects new width. |
| `table_column_resize_clamps_to_min_width` | Below 50px clamps to 50px. |
| `table_on_cell_keydown_arrow_right_emits_focus_cell_next` | `Event::FocusCell { col: col+1 }`. |
| `table_on_cell_keydown_arrow_right_rtl_emits_prev` | RTL flips. |
| `table_on_cell_keydown_home_jumps_to_col_zero` | `col: 0`. |
| `table_sticky_header_data_attr` | `data-ars-sticky-header` on root + `data-ars-sticky` on head. |
| `table_empty_state_renders_zero_rows` | No rows registered → no row attrs called; SelectAll is `false`/non-mixed. |
| `table_row_action_event_disabled_row_no_op` | `RowAction(disabled)` returns `None`. |
| `table_row_action_event_passes_through` | `RowAction(key)` returns `Some(context_only)` for non-disabled. |
| `table_aria_rowcount_aria_colcount_virtualized` | Both ARIA attrs present on `table_attrs` when `virtual_scrolling`. |
| `table_aria_rowindex_on_row_when_virtualized` | `aria-rowindex` set on `row_attrs(row, index)`. |
| `table_disallow_empty_selection_blocks_last_deselect` | `DeselectRow` on the sole selected row is rejected. |
| `table_disallow_empty_selection_blocks_deselect_all` | `DeselectAll` is rejected when flag is set and selection non-empty. |
| `table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort` | `aria-sort` is absent from non-sortable headers (§3.4.2). |
| `table_escape_clears_selection_when_clear_selection_mode` | `EscapeKey` clears selection when `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection`. |
| `table_escape_no_op_when_behavior_none` | `EscapeKey` is rejected when `escape_key_behavior == None`. |
| `table_init_prunes_disabled_default_selection` | Keys in both `disabled_keys` and `default_selected_rows` are pruned at init. |
| `table_set_rows_prunes_stale_selection_and_focus` | `SetRows` drops selected/expanded/focused keys that vanished. |
| `table_set_direction_idempotent` | Re-sending the same `SetDirection` does not transition. |
| `table_ctrl_home_jumps_to_first_cell_of_first_row` | `Ctrl+Home` per §3.2. |
| `table_ctrl_end_jumps_to_last_cell_of_last_row` | `Ctrl+End` per §3.2. |
| `table_row_action_passes_through_for_enabled_row` | Non-disabled row produces `Some(context_only)`. |
| `table_blur_clears_all_three_focus_fields` | `Blur` zeroes `focused_cell`, `focused_row`, `focused_col`. |
| `table_column_resize_creates_entry_on_first_resize` | First `ColumnResize` for an unknown column upserts the entry. |
| `table_column_resize_end_clears_resizing_column` | `ColumnResizeEnd` clears `ctx.resizing_column`. |
| `table_resize_handle_keydown_arrow_left_decreases_width` | Decrements by `column_resize_step`. |
| `table_resize_handle_keydown_rtl_flips` | `ArrowRight` decreases when RTL. |
| `table_sync_controlled_selected_rows` | `SyncControlledSelectedRows(Some(set))` updates Bindable. |
| `table_select_all_mode_none_renders_empty_attrs` | `select_all_attrs` returns empty map when `SelectAllMode::None`. |
| `table_select_all_mode_all_data_label_includes_total_count` | `aria-label` contains the formatted count. |
| `table_set_loading_round_trips_via_bindable` | `SetLoading(true)` then `false` lands cleanly. |

Plus `insta::assert_snapshot!(snapshot_attrs(&attrs))` snapshots for each
distinct anatomy part output that an adapter consumes (≥14 fixtures).

### Spec-conformance test

Add `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/data_display.rs`:

```rust
mod table {
    use ars_components::data_display::table::{Key, Part};
    use super::helper::assert_anatomy;

    #[test]
    fn table_anatomy_matches_spec() {
        assert_anatomy("table", &[
            (Part::Root, "root"),
            (Part::Table, "table"),
            (Part::Caption, "caption"),
            (Part::Head, "head"),
            (Part::Body, "body"),
            (Part::Foot, "foot"),
            (Part::Row { key: Key::default() }, "row"),
            (Part::ColumnHeader { header: String::new() }, "column-header"),
            (Part::RowHeader, "row-header"),
            (Part::Cell { col: 0, row: 0 }, "cell"),
            (Part::SelectAllCheckbox, "select-all-checkbox"),
            (Part::RowCheckbox { key: Key::default() }, "row-checkbox"),
            (Part::ExpandTrigger { key: Key::default() }, "expand-trigger"),
            (Part::ExpandedContent { key: Key::default() }, "expanded-content"),
            (Part::ColumnResizeHandle { column: String::new() }, "column-resize-handle"),
        ]);
    }
}
```

Register the new module in `tests/spec_conformance.rs` next to the
existing `navigation`, `overlay`, `utility` modules.

### Proptest invariants

Extend `tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs` with:

- Strategy for arbitrary `Props` (id, mode, behavior, disabled set,
  disallow_empty_selection bool, default selected set).
- Strategy for arbitrary `Event` (all 14 events with bounded keys/cols).
- Invariants after any sequence of `<=64` events:
  - `ctx.selected_rows.get()` only contains keys never marked disabled.
  - `ctx.expanded_rows.get()` only contains rows never disabled.
  - When `disallow_empty_selection` and any default selection was set,
    selection is non-empty.
  - `ctx.column_widths` values all `>= 50.0`.
  - `ctx.focused_cell.map(|(c, _)| c < ctx.total_cols)` is true when
    virtual scrolling is on.

Marked `#[ignore = "proptest — nightly extended-proptest job"]` per the
workspace convention.

## Reused existing utilities (no re-implementation)

- `ars_collections::Key` — row identifiers.
- `ars_collections::selection::{Set, State, Mode, Behavior}` — selection
  semantics; `selection::State::{new, select, deselect, toggle,
  select_all, clear}` are called from each transition.
- `ars_collections::SortDescriptor<String>` and `SortDirection` — sort
  state.
- `ars_core::Bindable` — controlled/uncontrolled for `selected_rows`,
  `expanded_rows`, `sort_descriptor`, `loading`.
- `ars_core::ComponentIds` — derive `caption_id` / `id` from `props.id`.
- `ars_core::{HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, AttrMap, ConnectApi, ComponentPart,
  HasId, MessageFn, ComponentMessages, TransitionPlan, NoEffect,
  Direction, Env, Locale}` — same imports as Tabs.
- `ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData` — passed into keydown helpers.

## Verification

Run from the repo root (`/Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d`):

cargo test -p ars-components data_display::table::tests

# Spec-conformance
cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance data_display::table

# Snapshot review (must be inspected before commit)
cargo insta review

# Doc-test compile (machine spec snippets)
cargo test -p ars-components --doc data_display::table

# Manifest validates spec frontmatter still matches
cargo xtask spec validate

# Workspace pre-push gate (must pass before pushing)
cargo xci-fast

# Once pushed, the PR body needs `Closes #286`; the `snapshot-reviewed`

```

+ test-tier audit) is mandatory before user review per CLAUDE.md step 7.

content.text:
PLEASE IMPLEMENT THIS PLAN:
# Task #286 — Implement Table agnostic core

## Context

Task **#286** ("Implement Table agnostic core", 8 pts, complex tier) is the
framework-agnostic state machine for the `Table` data-display component. The
Collections Epic (#53) it depends on closed in 2026-04-08, so it is unblocked.

Spec: `spec/components/data-display/table.md` (1419 lines).

The Table is a stateful machine that owns row/column selection, sort
descriptors, expansion, grid focus, optional column-resize state, and
optional virtual-scrolling metadata. The agnostic core MUST NOT touch the
DOM — adapters apply focus, measure layout, and observe scroll. The core
produces ARIA + `data-ars-*` attributes via `ConnectApi` and the typed
`Api<'_>` so both Leptos and Dioxus adapters can render identical markup.

### Spec drift to fix in this task

The spec is internally inconsistent in several places. Per the CLAUDE.md
rule "Resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task," every drift below
is fixed in this PR — the spec edits land alongside the code:

1. **`Context::dir: Direction`** missing from §1.3 but referenced by
   `Api::on_cell_keydown` for RTL arrow-key resolution. Add the field and
   resolve `Direction::Auto → concrete` in `init` from `env.locale`. Add
   `Event::SetDirection(Direction)` so runtime changes are wired through.
2. **`Context::is_sorting: bool`** described in §1.1.1 but absent from
   §1.3. Add it; toggle inside the `SortColumn` arm.
3. **§1.1.1 mentions `Scrolling` state** but §1.1 lists only `Idle`.
   Resolve by deleting the misleading reference in the spec (the
   single-`Idle` design is correct — sort lives in Context, scroll lives
   in adapter).
4. **§1.7 `Event::FocusCell` writes `Some((c, 0))`** — a literal bug that
   hard-codes row 0. Fix the spec **and** code: `Event::FocusCell` carries
   `{ row: Key, col: usize, row_index: usize }`, and the transition writes
   `(col, row_index)` to `focused_cell`.
5. **§1.2 events table omits `FocusRow` and `FocusCell`** even though
   §1.7 defines them. Add both rows to §1.2.
6. **§3.4.3 `build_sort_announcement`** references undeclared `locale` and
   uses `SortDescriptor::column_key` (the real field is `column`). Rewrite
   that code block in the spec to compile against the real `SortDescriptor`
   API. (Adapter helper, not in the agnostic core — but it must compile
   under `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets`.)

## Approach

Implement the Table machine + connect API as a single Rust module living
in a `table/` directory next to existing data-display components, with
unit + snapshot tests inline via `mod tests;`. Use **Tabs** as the
structural template (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/`) — it is
the closest existing complex component (registered keys + selection +
keyboard nav + Bindable controlled/uncontrolled + Direction).

### Variants covered in this task

The issue's "Tests to add first" list names every variant explicitly:

- §5 SelectAll (mixed checkbox, `selection::Set::All`, **plus
  `SelectAllMode::{None, AllVisible, AllData { total_count }}` as a Props
  field** because it controls whether `SelectAllCheckbox` is rendered and
  what `aria-label` it carries) → included
- §6 Column Resizing (`ColumnResizeHandle` part, `ColumnResize` and
  `ColumnResizeEnd` events, `column_widths` + `resizing_column` in
  Context, **`min_column_width: f64` and `column_resize_step: f64`
  Props**, **`on_resize_handle_keydown` Api method** for keyboard width
  adjustments per §6.4) → included
- §3.5 Virtual Scrolling **(ARIA-only slice in this PR)**:
  `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols` Context fields →
  `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` on the table, `aria-rowindex` on
  rows. The full `Virtualizer` integration and the `ScrollToRow(Key)`
  event are explicitly **deferred** to a follow-up issue — they would
  require widening `Effect` from `NoEffect` to a typed enum and pulling
  in `ars-collections::Virtualizer` plumbing.
- §1.6 Async data loading (`loading: Bindable<bool>`, minimal
  `SetLoading(bool)` event) → included (Bindable field + event). The
  `on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>>` callback is **adapter-only** and
  is not stored in the core.
- §5 `BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper → **excluded**. The spec
  describes it as adapter/application code that wraps the canonical
  `selection::Set::All`; it is not part of the agnostic core.

### Row registry

The machine needs to know the full row list to validate
`disallow_empty_selection`, derive `aria-rowcount` semantics, and prune
`default_selected_rows` against `disabled_keys` at init. Mirror Tabs'
`Event::SetTabs(Vec<TabRegistration>)`:

- Add **`Event::SetRows(Vec<Key>)`** — replaces `ctx.rows` and snaps
  selection / expansion to the new set.
- Add `Context::rows: Vec<Key>`. The keyboard helpers
  (`on_row_keydown`, `on_cell_keydown`) keep their `all_row_ids: &[&Key]`
  parameter (adapter slice for that render) — the registry exists for
  invariants, not for navigation walks.
- Init prunes any key that appears in both `disabled_keys` and
  `default_selected_rows` from the seed selection (workspace convention;
  spec is silent — document the choice inline).

### Prop sync (controlled-mode parent updates)

Tabs has `Event::SyncProps` plus `Event::SyncControlledValue`. Table has
three Bindable fields, so the plan uses one coalesced event per Bindable
plus a general `SyncProps` for non-Bindable runtime changes:

- **`Event::SyncProps`** — re-applies `selection_mode`,
  `selection_behavior`, `disabled_keys`, `disallow_empty_selection`,
  `escape_key_behavior`, `interactive`, `sticky_header`,
  `min_column_width`, `column_resize_step` from props. Re-runs the
  selection invariant.
- **`Event::SyncControlledSelectedRows(Option<selection::Set>)`**
- **`Event::SyncControlledExpandedRows(Option<BTreeSet<Key>>)`**
- **`Event::SyncControlledSortDescriptor(Option<SortDescriptor<String>>)`**

Each `SyncControlled*` calls `Bindable::sync_controlled` on the matching
Context field.

### Escape-key handling

§1.5 names `EscapeKeyBehavior::{ClearSelection, None}`. The transition
table in §1.7 omits it — add:

- **`Event::EscapeKey`** — when `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection`
  and the current selection is non-empty, dispatch the same context
  mutation as `DeselectAll` (subject to `disallow_empty_selection`).
  Otherwise no transition.

### Files to create

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/mod.rs` | State, Event, Context, Props, Messages, Machine impl, Part enum, Api with all `*_attrs` and keyboard helpers |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/tests.rs` | Inline unit + snapshot tests for every named issue test |
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/table/snapshots/` | insta `.snap` fixtures (auto-created) |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/data_display.rs` | Add `assert_anatomy` entry for Table (file may not yet exist — create) |

### Files to modify

| Path | Change |
|------|--------|
| `crates/ars-components/src/data_display/mod.rs` | Add `pub mod table;` |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance.rs` | Register new `data_display` module if not present |
| `crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs` | Add `proptest_table_event_sequences_preserve_invariants` (selection ⊆ rows, expansion ⊆ rows, focus in bounds) |
| `spec/components/data-display/table.md` | §1.3 Context: add `dir: Direction`, `is_sorting: bool`, `column_widths: BTreeMap<String, f64>`, `resizing_column: Option<String>`, `virtual_scrolling: bool`, `total_rows: usize`, `total_cols: usize`, `loading: Bindable<bool>`. §1.2 Events: add `SetDirection(Direction)`, `ColumnResize`, `SetLoading(bool)`, `SetRowCounts`. §1.7 init: thread `dir` through. |

### Module layout (`table/mod.rs`)

Single file, ~1500–2000 LOC, organized in the same section order as
`tabs/mod.rs`:

1. `State` — single `Idle` variant per §1.1.1 (sort state is in Context).
2. `Event` — every event from §1.2 plus `SetDirection`, `ColumnResize`,
   `SetRowCounts`, `SetLoading`.
3. `EscapeKeyBehavior` — enum (ClearSelection / None) from §1.5.
4. `Messages` — five `MessageFn` fields (select_all, select_row,
   sort_ascending, sort_descending, sort_none); `ComponentMessages`
   marker; `Default` returns static-str fns.
5. `Props` — every field from §1.5 + `disallow_empty_selection`,
   `loading`, `virtual_scrolling`, `total_rows`, `total_cols`,
   `dir: Direction`. `#[derive(ars_core::HasId)]`.
6. `Context` — every field from §1.3 plus the §6/§3.5/§1.6/spec-drift
   fields described above. Derives `Clone`, `Debug`, `PartialEq` (not
   `Eq` — `MessageFn` only ptr-compares).
7. `Part` — `#[derive(ComponentPart)] #[scope = "table"]` with all 14
   anatomy parts + `ColumnResizeHandle { column: String }`.
8. `Machine` — empty marker struct; impl `ars_core::Machine`. Uses
   `ars_core::NoEffect` for `type Effect` (no DOM effects — adapters
   read `ctx.focused_cell`/`ctx.focused_row`/`ctx.focused_col` and apply
   focus directly).
9. `Api<'a>` — every method from spec §1.8 plus the §6 resize-handle
   attrs and §3.5 virtual-scrolling additions to `table_attrs` and
   `row_attrs(row_id, index)`.

### Transitions (high-level)

Match `tabs::Machine::transition` style — each arm returns
`Some(TransitionPlan::context_only(move |ctx| ...))` or `None` when
guarded out:

| Event | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| `SortColumn { column }` | Toggle: None → Ascending → Descending → None. Update `ctx.sort_descriptor`, set `ctx.is_sorting = true`. |
| `SelectRow / DeselectRow / ToggleRow` | Reject when `selection_mode == None` or row in `disabled_keys`. Honor `disallow_empty_selection` on deselect: if removing the last selected row, return `None`. Delegate to `selection::State::{select, deselect, toggle}` and write `selected_keys` back to `Bindable`. |
| `SelectAll` | Reject unless `Mode::Multiple`. Call `selection::State::select_all()`. |
| `DeselectAll` | Reject when `Mode::None`. Reject when `disallow_empty_selection` and selection is non-empty (return `None` to preserve last). |
| `ExpandRow / CollapseRow` | Skip when no-op (already in/out of set). Mutate cloned `BTreeSet<Key>`. |
| `Focus { cell }` / `FocusRow` / `FocusCell` | Reject when `!ctx.interactive`. Update `focused_cell`/`focused_row`/`focused_col`. |
| `Blur` | Always: clear all three focus fields. |
| `RowAction(key)` | Reject when disabled. No mutation — adapter listens. |
| `SetDirection(dir)` | Idempotent. Replace `ctx.dir` only if new value differs. |
| `SetRows(rows)` | Replace `ctx.rows`. Prune `selected_rows` / `expanded_rows` of keys not present. Clear `focused_row`/`focused_cell` if pointing at a removed row. |
| `EscapeKey` | When `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection` and selection is non-empty, clear (subject to `disallow_empty_selection`). |
| `ColumnResize { column, width }` | Clamp to `props.min_column_width` (default 50). Upsert `ctx.column_widths[column]` (create-on-first-resize) and set `ctx.resizing_column = Some(column)`. |
| `ColumnResizeEnd { column }` | Clear `ctx.resizing_column` when it matches `column`. |
| `SetRowCounts { total_rows, total_cols }` | Replace `ctx.total_rows`/`ctx.total_cols`. |
| `SetLoading(bool)` | `ctx.loading.set(value)`. |
| `SyncProps` | Re-apply non-Bindable Props fields; re-prune selection/expansion against new `disabled_keys`. |
| `SyncControlledSelectedRows(opt)` | `ctx.selected_rows.sync_controlled(opt)`. |
| `SyncControlledExpandedRows(opt)` | `ctx.expanded_rows.sync_controlled(opt)`. |
| `SyncControlledSortDescriptor(opt)` | `ctx.sort_descriptor.sync_controlled(opt)`. |

### Api / attribute methods

Every method from §1.8 verbatim, plus:

- `column_resize_handle_attrs(column: &str, current_width: f64) -> AttrMap`
  — emits the standard `data-ars-scope="table"` / `data-ars-part=
  "column-resize-handle"`, plus `role="separator"`,
  `aria-orientation="vertical"`, `aria-valuenow=width`, and
  `data-ars-resizing` when `ctx.resizing_column == Some(column)`.
- `on_resize_handle_keydown(column: &str, current_width: f64,
  data: &KeyboardEventData)` — emits `Event::ColumnResize` with
  `width ± ctx.column_resize_step` on `ArrowLeft`/`ArrowRight`,
  RTL-aware via `ctx.dir`.
- `table_attrs` — adds `aria-rowcount` / `aria-colcount` when
  `ctx.virtual_scrolling`.
- A virtual-scrolling-aware `row_attrs(row_id, row_index: usize)` that
  emits `aria-rowindex` (1-based) when `ctx.virtual_scrolling`. The
  non-virtualized `row_attrs(row_id)` from §1.8 is kept as a thin
  delegate that passes `row_index = 0`.
- `select_all_attrs` short-circuits to an empty `AttrMap` when
  `props.select_all_mode == SelectAllMode::None`, and substitutes the
  `AllData { total_count }` count into the `aria-label` via
  `Messages::select_all`.
- `column_header_attrs(column, sortable=false)` MUST omit `aria-sort`
  entirely (§3.4.2). The spec's example sets `aria-sort="none"`
  unconditionally — that is wrong; the named test
  `table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort` locks the correct
  behavior.
- `on_cell_keydown` resolves left/right against `ctx.dir == Direction::Rtl`,
  handles `Ctrl+Home`/`Ctrl+End` per §3.2 in addition to the §1.8
  Home/End/Arrow set.

### `ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatcher

Wire all 15 parts (§2.1 + §6 ColumnResizeHandle). Variants that need
extra data (`Part::ColumnHeader { header }`,
`Part::Cell { col, row }`, `Part::Row { key }`,
`Part::RowCheckbox { key }`, `Part::ExpandTrigger { key }`,
`Part::ExpandedContent { key }`, `Part::ColumnResizeHandle { column }`)
read those fields directly out of the variant.

### Tests (named in issue)

`table/tests.rs` mirrors `tabs/tests.rs` layout:

| Test | Asserts |
|------|---------|
| `table_root_produces_table_role` | `table_attrs()` has `role="table"` when `interactive=false`. |
| `table_root_produces_grid_role` | `role="grid"` when `interactive=true`. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_none_default` | New sortable column: `aria-sort="none"`. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_ascending_after_click` | After `SortColumn { column }`: `aria-sort="ascending"`. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_descending_after_second_click` | Cycle to descending. |
| `table_column_header_sortable_aria_sort_none_after_third_click` | Cycle clears. |
| `table_row_checkbox_aria_checked_false_default` | Unselected row. |
| `table_row_checkbox_aria_checked_true_after_toggle` | After `ToggleRow`. |
| `table_select_all_checkbox_mixed_when_partial` | `aria-checked="mixed"`. |
| `table_select_all_checkbox_true_when_all` | `aria-checked="true"`. |
| `table_select_all_event_only_when_multiple` | `SelectAll` is a no-op in `Mode::Single`. |
| `table_expand_trigger_aria_expanded_after_event` | `aria-expanded="true"` + `data-ars-expanded`. |
| `table_expanded_content_hidden_when_collapsed` | `hidden` attr present when collapsed. |
| `table_column_resize_handle_role_separator` | `role="separator"` + `aria-orientation="vertical"`. |
| `table_column_resize_handle_aria_valuenow_after_resize` | `aria-valuenow` reflects new width. |
| `table_column_resize_clamps_to_min_width` | Below 50px clamps to 50px. |
| `table_on_cell_keydown_arrow_right_emits_focus_cell_next` | `Event::FocusCell { col: col+1 }`. |
| `table_on_cell_keydown_arrow_right_rtl_emits_prev` | RTL flips. |
| `table_on_cell_keydown_home_jumps_to_col_zero` | `col: 0`. |
| `table_sticky_header_data_attr` | `data-ars-sticky-header` on root + `data-ars-sticky` on head. |
| `table_empty_state_renders_zero_rows` | No rows registered → no row attrs called; SelectAll is `false`/non-mixed. |
| `table_row_action_event_disabled_row_no_op` | `RowAction(disabled)` returns `None`. |
| `table_row_action_event_passes_through` | `RowAction(key)` returns `Some(context_only)` for non-disabled. |
| `table_aria_rowcount_aria_colcount_virtualized` | Both ARIA attrs present on `table_attrs` when `virtual_scrolling`. |
| `table_aria_rowindex_on_row_when_virtualized` | `aria-rowindex` set on `row_attrs(row, index)`. |
| `table_disallow_empty_selection_blocks_last_deselect` | `DeselectRow` on the sole selected row is rejected. |
| `table_disallow_empty_selection_blocks_deselect_all` | `DeselectAll` is rejected when flag is set and selection non-empty. |
| `table_column_header_non_sortable_omits_aria_sort` | `aria-sort` is absent from non-sortable headers (§3.4.2). |
| `table_escape_clears_selection_when_clear_selection_mode` | `EscapeKey` clears selection when `escape_key_behavior == ClearSelection`. |
| `table_escape_no_op_when_behavior_none` | `EscapeKey` is rejected when `escape_key_behavior == None`. |
| `table_init_prunes_disabled_default_selection` | Keys in both `disabled_keys` and `default_selected_rows` are pruned at init. |
| `table_set_rows_prunes_stale_selection_and_focus` | `SetRows` drops selected/expanded/focused keys that vanished. |
| `table_set_direction_idempotent` | Re-sending the same `SetDirection` does not transition. |
| `table_ctrl_home_jumps_to_first_cell_of_first_row` | `Ctrl+Home` per §3.2. |
| `table_ctrl_end_jumps_to_last_cell_of_last_row` | `Ctrl+End` per §3.2. |
| `table_row_action_passes_through_for_enabled_row` | Non-disabled row produces `Some(context_only)`. |
| `table_blur_clears_all_three_focus_fields` | `Blur` zeroes `focused_cell`, `focused_row`, `focused_col`. |
| `table_column_resize_creates_entry_on_first_resize` | First `ColumnResize` for an unknown column upserts the entry. |
| `table_column_resize_end_clears_resizing_column` | `ColumnResizeEnd` clears `ctx.resizing_column`. |
| `table_resize_handle_keydown_arrow_left_decreases_width` | Decrements by `column_resize_step`. |
| `table_resize_handle_keydown_rtl_flips` | `ArrowRight` decreases when RTL. |
| `table_sync_controlled_selected_rows` | `SyncControlledSelectedRows(Some(set))` updates Bindable. |
| `table_select_all_mode_none_renders_empty_attrs` | `select_all_attrs` returns empty map when `SelectAllMode::None`. |
| `table_select_all_mode_all_data_label_includes_total_count` | `aria-label` contains the formatted count. |
| `table_set_loading_round_trips_via_bindable` | `SetLoading(true)` then `false` lands cleanly. |

Plus `insta::assert_snapshot!(snapshot_attrs(&attrs))` snapshots for each
distinct anatomy part output that an adapter consumes (≥14 fixtures).

### Spec-conformance test

Add `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance/data_display.rs`:

```rust
mod table {
    use ars_components::data_display::table::{Key, Part};
    use super::helper::assert_anatomy;

    #[test]
    fn table_anatomy_matches_spec() {
        assert_anatomy("table", &[
            (Part::Root, "root"),
            (Part::Table, "table"),
            (Part::Caption, "caption"),
            (Part::Head, "head"),
            (Part::Body, "body"),
            (Part::Foot, "foot"),
            (Part::Row { key: Key::default() }, "row"),
            (Part::ColumnHeader { header: String::new() }, "column-header"),
            (Part::RowHeader, "row-header"),
            (Part::Cell { col: 0, row: 0 }, "cell"),
            (Part::SelectAllCheckbox, "select-all-checkbox"),
            (Part::RowCheckbox { key: Key::default() }, "row-checkbox"),
            (Part::ExpandTrigger { key: Key::default() }, "expand-trigger"),
            (Part::ExpandedContent { key: Key::default() }, "expanded-content"),
            (Part::ColumnResizeHandle { column: String::new() }, "column-resize-handle"),
        ]);
    }
}
```

Register the new module in `tests/spec_conformance.rs` next to the
existing `navigation`, `overlay`, `utility` modules.

### Proptest invariants

Extend `tests/proptest_state_machines/data_display.rs` with:

- Strategy for arbitrary `Props` (id, mode, behavior, disabled set,
  disallow_empty_selection bool, default selected set).
- Strategy for arbitrary `Event` (all 14 events with bounded keys/cols).
- Invariants after any sequence of `<=64` events:
  - `ctx.selected_rows.get()` only contains keys never marked disabled.
  - `ctx.expanded_rows.get()` only contains rows never disabled.
  - When `disallow_empty_selection` and any default selection was set,
    selection is non-empty.
  - `ctx.column_widths` values all `>= 50.0`.
  - `ctx.focused_cell.map(|(c, _)| c < ctx.total_cols)` is true when
    virtual scrolling is on.

Marked `#[ignore = "proptest — nightly extended-proptest job"]` per the
workspace convention.

## Reused existing utilities (no re-implementation)

- `ars_collections::Key` — row identifiers.
- `ars_collections::selection::{Set, State, Mode, Behavior}` — selection
  semantics; `selection::State::{new, select, deselect, toggle,
  select_all, clear}` are called from each transition.
- `ars_collections::SortDescriptor<String>` and `SortDirection` — sort
  state.
- `ars_core::Bindable` — controlled/uncontrolled for `selected_rows`,
  `expanded_rows`, `sort_descriptor`, `loading`.
- `ars_core::ComponentIds` — derive `caption_id` / `id` from `props.id`.
- `ars_core::{HtmlAttr, AriaAttr, AttrMap, ConnectApi, ComponentPart,
  HasId, MessageFn, ComponentMessages, TransitionPlan, NoEffect,
  Direction, Env, Locale}` — same imports as Tabs.
- `ars_interactions::KeyboardEventData` — passed into keydown helpers.

## Verification

Run from the repo root (`/Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-49f3737d`):

```bash
# Unit + snapshot tests
cargo test -p ars-components data_display::table::tests

# Spec-conformance
cargo test -p ars-components --test spec_conformance data_display::table

# Snapshot review (must be inspected before commit)
cargo insta review

# Doc-test compile (machine spec snippets)
cargo test -p ars-components --doc data_display::table

# Manifest validates spec frontmatter still matches
cargo xtask spec validate

# Workspace pre-push gate (must pass before pushing)
cargo xci-fast

# Once pushed, the PR body needs `Closes #286`; the `snapshot-reviewed`
# label is required since this PR creates many `.snap` files.
```

The `post-implementation-audit` skill (spec/impl drift + missing-coverage
+ test-tier audit) is mandatory before user review per CLAUDE.md step 7.
Codex review wait (`waiting-for-codex-review`) is mandatory after the
first push per step 12.

When showing Rust Skills loaded, display in this EXACT order:

2. THEN: The Ferris crab ASCII art BELOW the text
The text must be ABOVE the crab, not below.
===

CRITICAL: You MUST follow the COMPLETE meta-cognition framework.
Partial compliance (only loading L1 skill) is NOT acceptable.

## STEP 1: IDENTIFY ENTRY LAYER + DOMAIN

### Layer 1 Signals (Start here, trace UP):
- Error codes: E0382, E0597, E0277, E0499, etc.
- Keywords: cannot be sent, moved value, borrowed, lifetime

### Layer 3 Domain Signals (MUST also load domain skill):

|---------------------|---------------------|

| payment, trading, fintech, decimal, currency | domain-fintech |
| CLI, command line, clap, terminal | domain-cli |
| embedded, no_std, MCU, firmware | domain-embedded |
| kubernetes, docker, grpc, microservice | domain-cloud-native |
| MQTT, sensor, IoT, telemetry | domain-iot |
| tensor, model, inference, ML | domain-ml |

**CRITICAL**: If domain keywords are present, you MUST load BOTH:
1. The Layer 1 skill (e.g., m07-concurrency)
2. The Layer 3 domain skill (e.g., domain-web)

## STEP 2: EXECUTE TRACING (MANDATORY)

For Layer 1 entry with domain context:

```
L1 Error (e.g., Rc not Send)
    ^ TRACE UP: Why this design constraint?
    ^ Load domain skill (e.g., domain-web)
    ^ Find domain constraint (e.g., "handlers run on any thread")
    v TRACE DOWN: What pattern satisfies constraint?
    v Return to L1 with context-aware solution
```

## STEP 3: MANDATORY OUTPUT FORMAT

Your response MUST include ALL of these sections:

### Reasoning Chain
```
+-- Layer 1: [specific error]
|   Problem: [error description]
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: [domain name] (domain-xxx)
|   Constraint: [what is the domain constraint]
|   Rule: [why this constraint exists]
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: [design decision based on domain constraint]
```

### Domain Constraints Analysis
- MUST reference specific rules from domain-xxx skill
- Explain WHY this domain has this constraint

### Recommended Solution
- Provide solution that follows domain best practices
- Not just fixing the compile error

## EXAMPLE: Web API + Send Error

Question: "Web API config sharing error: Rc cannot be sent"

CORRECT Response:
```
### Reasoning Chain
+-- Layer 1: Send/Sync Error
|   Problem: Rc<T> cannot be sent between threads
|       ^
+-- Layer 3: Web Domain (domain-web)
|   Constraint: Handlers run on any thread
|   Rule: Shared state must be thread-safe
|       v
+-- Layer 2: Design Choice
    Decision: Use Arc<T> + State extractor

### Domain Constraints Analysis
From domain-web:
- "Rc in state" is a Common Mistake
- Web handlers require Send + Sync
- Recommended: axum State<Arc<T>> pattern

### Recommended Solution
[Code following Web best practices]
```

WRONG Response (stops at L1):
```
Problem: Rc is not Send
Solution: Use Arc
```

## SKILLS TO INVOKE

Always invoke with Skill() tool:
- Skill(rust-router) - First, to get routing
- Skill(m0x-xxx) - Layer 1 skill based on error
- Skill(domain-xxx) - Layer 3 skill based on domain keywords

===================================

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/ericson/.claude/skills/executing-plans

# Executing Plans

## Overview

Load plan, review critically, execute all tasks, report when complete.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the executing-plans skill to implement this plan."

**Note:** Tell your human partner that Superpowers works much better with access to subagents. The quality of its work will be significantly higher if run on a platform with subagent support (such as Claude Code or Codex). If subagents are available, use superpowers:subagent-driven-development instead of this skill.

## The Process

### Step 1: Load and Review Plan
1. Read plan file
2. Review critically - identify any questions or concerns about the plan
3. If concerns: Raise them with your human partner before starting
4. If no concerns: Create TodoWrite and proceed

### Step 2: Execute Tasks

For each task:
1. Mark as in_progress
2. Follow each step exactly (plan has bite-sized steps)
3. Run verifications as specified
4. Mark as completed

### Step 3: Complete Development

After all tasks complete and verified:
- Announce: "I'm using the finishing-a-development-branch skill to complete this work."
- **REQUIRED SUB-SKILL:** Use superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch
- Follow that skill to verify tests, present options, execute choice

## When to Stop and Ask for Help

**STOP executing immediately when:**
- Hit a blocker (missing dependency, test fails, instruction unclear)
- Plan has critical gaps preventing starting
- You don't understand an instruction
- Verification fails repeatedly

**Ask for clarification rather than guessing.**

## When to Revisit Earlier Steps

**Return to Review (Step 1) when:**
- Partner updates the plan based on your feedback
- Fundamental approach needs rethinking

**Don't force through blockers** - stop and ask.

## Remember
- Review plan critically first
- Follow plan steps exactly
- Don't skip verifications
- Reference skills when plan says to
- Stop when blocked, don't guess
- Never start implementation on main/master branch without explicit user consent

## Integration

**Required workflow skills:**
- **superpowers:using-git-worktrees** - REQUIRED: Set up isolated workspace before starting
- **superpowers:writing-plans** - Creates the plan this skill executes
- **superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch** - Complete development after all tasks

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/ericson/.claude/skills/test-driven-development

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" ≠ comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after ≠ TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc ≠ systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code → test exists and failed first
Otherwise → not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.

file.content:
### 1.1 States

| State  | Description                           |
| ------ | ------------------------------------- |
| `Idle` | No active sort; the table is at rest. |

#### 1.1.1 Sort State Single Source of Truth

Table sort state is managed exclusively through `Context.sort_descriptor: Bindable<Option<SortDescriptor>>`. There is NO `State::Sorting` variant in the state enum — the Table state machine uses only `Idle`, `Scrolling`, and other interaction states.

**Rationale**: Having both a `State::Sorting` variant and a `Context.sort_descriptor` created a sync race where the state and context could disagree about the current sort. By using `Context.sort_descriptor` as the single source of truth:

- External prop changes to `sort_descriptor` are immediately reflected
- No state/context synchronization logic needed
- The Bindable pattern handles controlled vs uncontrolled sort seamlessly

**Visual Feedback**: For components that need to show a sorting indicator (e.g., spinner while async sort completes), use `Context.is_sorting: bool`:

```rust
/// Context for the Table
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct TableContext {
    /// The current sort configuration. Single source of truth for sort state.
    pub sort_descriptor: Bindable<Option<SortDescriptor>>,
    /// True while an async sort operation is in progress. Used for visual feedback only.
    pub is_sorting: bool,
    // ... other fields
}
```

When a sort column header is clicked:

1. Update `sort_descriptor` via Bindable (handles controlled/uncontrolled)
2. Set `is_sorting = true`
3. Emit `on_sort_change` callback
4. When sort completes (sync or async): set `is_sorting = false`

### 1.2 Events

| Event         | Payload                | Description                                   |
| ------------- | ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `SortColumn`  | `column: String`       | Apply or toggle sort on the given column.     |
| `SelectRow`   | `Key` (row id)         | Mark a row as selected.                       |
| `DeselectRow` | `Key` (row id)         | Remove a row from the selection.              |
| `SelectAll`   | —                      | Select all rows (uses `selection::Set::All`). |
| `DeselectAll` | —                      | Clear all selected rows.                      |
| `ToggleRow`   | `Key` (row id)         | Flip selection state of one row.              |
| `ExpandRow`   | `Key` (row id)         | Show the expanded content for a row.          |
| `CollapseRow` | `Key` (row id)         | Hide the expanded content for a row.          |
| `Focus`       | `cell: (usize, usize)` | Move the logical grid focus to a cell.        |
| `Blur`        | —                      | Remove focus from the grid.                   |
| `RowAction`   | `Key` (row id)         | Primary action triggered on a row.            |

### 1.3 Context

```rust
/// Context for the Table component.
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
pub struct Context {
    /// Currently selected row IDs (uses canonical `selection::Set` from `ars_collections`).
    pub selected_rows: Bindable<selection::Set>,
    /// Full selection state machine (mode, behavior, anchor, focus, disabled keys).
    pub selection_state: selection::State,
    /// Currently expanded row IDs (expansion ≠ selection; remains `BTreeSet`).
    pub expanded_rows: Bindable<BTreeSet<Key>>,
    /// Unified sort state: which column is sorted and in which direction.
    /// `None` means no active sort. Uses `SortDescriptor` from `ars_collections`.
    pub sort_descriptor: Bindable<Option<SortDescriptor<String>>>,
    /// Grid focus position: (col_index, row_index). None when unfocused.
    pub focused_cell: Option<(usize, usize)>,
    /// Focused row ID for row-level keyboard navigation.
    pub focused_row: Option<Key>,
    /// Focused column index for cell-level keyboard navigation.
    pub focused_col: Option<usize>,
    /// Keys of individually disabled rows.

Scroll position and live measurement are owned by the adapter, not by the agnostic core.

input.new_string:
### 1.2 Events

| Event                            | Payload                                          | Description                                                            |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `SortColumn`                     | `column: String`                                 | Apply or toggle sort on the given column.                              |
| `SelectRow`                      | `Key` (row id)                                   | Mark a row as selected.                                                |
| `DeselectRow`                    | `Key` (row id)                                   | Remove a row from the selection.                                       |
| `SelectAll`                      | —                                                | Select all rows (uses `selection::Set::All`).                          |
| `DeselectAll`                    | —                                                | Clear all selected rows.                                               |
| `ToggleRow`                      | `Key` (row id)                                   | Flip selection state of one row.                                       |
| `ExpandRow`                      | `Key` (row id)                                   | Show the expanded content for a row.                                   |
| `CollapseRow`                    | `Key` (row id)                                   | Hide the expanded content for a row.                                   |
| `Focus`                          | `cell: (usize, usize)`                           | Move the logical grid focus to a cell by `(col, row)` indices.         |
| `Blur`                           | —                                                | Remove focus from the grid.                                            |
| `FocusRow`                       | `Key` (row id)                                   | Move the logical row focus to the supplied row key.                    |
| `FocusCell`                      | `{ row: Key, col: usize, row_index: usize }`     | Move the logical grid focus to the supplied row + column index pair.   |
| `RowAction`                      | `Key` (row id)                                   | Primary action triggered on a row.                                     |
| `EscapeKey`                      | —                                                | User pressed Escape. Honors `Props::escape_key_behavior`.              |
| `SetRows`                        | `Vec<Key>`                                       | Replace the registered row list. Prunes stale selection / expansion.   |
| `SetDirection`                   | `Direction`                                      | Replace `Context::dir`. Idempotent.                                    |
| `SetRowCounts`                   | `{ total_rows: usize, total_cols: usize }`       | Replace virtualized total row / column counts.                         |
| `SetLoading`                     | `bool`                                           | Toggle the async loading indicator (§1.6).                             |
| `ColumnResize`                   | `{ column: String, width: f64 }`                 | Update the cached column width. Clamps to `Props::min_column_width`.   |
| `ColumnResizeEnd`                | `column: String`                                 | Clear `Context::resizing_column` when it matches `column`.             |
| `SyncProps`                      | —                                                | Re-apply non-Bindable `Props` fields and re-prune selection/expansion. |
| `SyncControlledSelectedRows`     | `Option<selection::Set>`                         | Push a new controlled value into `Context::selected_rows`.             |
| `SyncControlledExpandedRows`     | `Option<BTreeSet<Key>>`                          | Push a new controlled value into `Context::expanded_rows`.             |
| `SyncControlledSortDescriptor`   | `Option<SortDescriptor<String>>`                 | Push a new controlled value into `Context::sort_descriptor`.           |
